symptom: ANC health check fails with DNS resolution error: '_ldap._tcp.domain.com: This operation returned because the timeout period expired DNS resolution failed for domain name'
rootCause: DC DNS SRV records (_ldap._tcp.domain.local) were manually added in the DNS Server Configuration, causing resolution failures
solution: Verify DNS Servers configured in the VNET are reachable, Domain Join ports are not blocked, and check if customer manually added DC DNS SRV records (_ldap._tcp.domain.local) in DNS Server - remove manual entries if present
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/ANC Check failing with DNS Resolution failed for domain name: _ldap._tcp

symptom: ANC deploy failure due to 'Environment and configuration is ready' check failing with VMExtensionProvisioningError/canarytest DSC extension failure
rootCause: GPO with 'AllSigned' execution policy for PowerShell blocks DSC extension, or required W365 endpoints are not whitelisted causing DSC configuration download failure
solution: Solution 1: Move ANC to blank GPO (remove AllSigned execution policy). Solution 2: Check Kusto with correlation ID for specific blocked URLs, whitelist required endpoints per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/requirements-network, verify with Azure VM on same subnet or ASC traffic test
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/ANC Deploy failure due to Environment and configuration is ready check

symptom: ANC configuration fails during Environment and configuration checks with InternalServerErrorUnableToRunDscScript - WinRM access blocked
rootCause: WinRM service incorrectly configured via image, GPO deployment, or Intune CSP configuration, blocking PowerShell/DSC execution
solution: Reconfigure WinRM service to allow messages from IP addresses. Check GPO: see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winrm/installation-and-configuration-for-windows-remote-management. Check Intune CSP: RemoteManagement Policy CSP. Use Kusto query with ANCID and TenantID to verify winrm failures in CloudPCEvent
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/ANC Failures relating to WinRM configuration

symptom: ANC endpoint connectivity check shows warning for http://login.microsoftonline.com due to HTTP 302 redirect to https://login.microsoftonline.com and then to www.office.com
rootCause: ANC checks both TCP and HTTP for login.microsoftonline.com; HTTP check encounters 302 redirections, and if customer blocks the redirected URL (www.office.com), the check fails with warning
solution: Whitelist www.office.com as workaround. Dev team and PM are aware and working on improvement. Use CPCD Action Diagnostic - ANC Health Check History to verify endpoint connectivity status
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/AzureNetworkConnection - ANC/Endpoint connectivity

symptom: Cannot delete unused Azure Network Connection (ANC) via Graph API - error 'Failed to delete the on-premises network connection as it is referenced in a provisioning policy' with inner code 'isInUse'
rootCause: Provisioning policy that previously referenced the ANC was deleted but backend retains stale reference to the ANC, preventing deletion
solution: 1) Run Kusto query on CloudPCEvent with TenantID and ANC ID to check if ANC is still referenced by a provisioning policy. 2) If policy ID returned is stale (no active policy in Intune portal or Graph API), raise ICM to SaaF team with TenantID, ANC ID, Graph API call details, error message, and Kusto evidence for backend cleanup
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/AzureNetworkConnection - ANC/Unused ANC cannot be deleted

symptom: ANC Endpoint Connectivity check fails each time for different endpoints - intermittent DNS resolution failures for various Microsoft endpoints
rootCause: DNS Conditional Forwarders on the Domain Controller only include main domains but not the CNAME/A record subdomains returned when accessing those endpoints; SNAT port exhaustion on firewall can also contribute
solution: 1) Collect trace logs from DC during ANC checks to identify all accessed endpoints and their CNAME/A records. 2) Add missing subdomains to Conditional Forwarders list. 3) Better approach: configure Conditional Forwarders only for internal websites and set main Forwarder to 168.63.129.16 for all other domains (avoids dynamic subdomain changes). 4) Check and resolve SNAT port exhaustion by acquiring additional public IPs
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/Endpoint Connectivity check fails each time for different endpoints

symptom: ANC health check fails with ResourceAvailabilityCheckNoSubnetIP - subnet has no available IP addresses, orphaned NICs visible in Azure Portal with no attached VM
rootCause: Delete Lock or ReadOnly Lock on the resource group prevents W365 DeleteAzureResourceService from removing orphaned NICs when Cloud PCs are deleted or reprovisioned
solution: 1) Go to Azure Portal > Resource Groups > affected RG > Settings > Locks, note lock type. 2) Identify orphaned NICs (Network Interfaces with blank VM column). 3) Remove the lock to allow W365 cleanup service to run. 4) Monitor NIC count until orphaned NICs are removed. 5) Reapply lock after cleanup if needed. Kusto: query CloudPCEvent for DeleteAzureResourceService + ScopeLocked errors
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/ResourceAvailabilityCheckNoSubnetIP - Orphaned NICs Not Cleaned Up Due to Resource Lock

symptom: Custom image upload fails in Intune Admin Center after Service Release 2503 - error instructs user to upload a new image without a data disk attached
rootCause: As of SR 2503 (March 31, 2025), adding a custom image fails if the source VM had an Azure Managed Disk attached as additional data disk when the image was created
solution: Create a new custom image ensuring the source VM does not have any additional managed disks attached before running Sysprep and sharing the image to Azure
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Custom Images/Custom Images with Managed Disks Not Supported

symptom: Settings > Accounts > Windows Backup crashes on Cloud PCs provisioned with Windows 365 optimized image
rootCause: Connected Devices Platform Service (cdpsvc) is disabled in the W365 optimized image, causing Windows Backup settings page to crash
solution: Re-enable the service via registry and reboot: 1) Set-ItemProperty -Name Start -Value 2 -Force -Path HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\CDPUserSvc, HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\CDPSvc  2) Shutdown -t 0 -r  3) Get-Service CDP* | Start-Service -Verbose
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Custom Images/Windows Backup setting crashes on Windows 365 optimized image

symptom: Windows 365 web portal (windows365.microsoft.com) displays wrong language for Hybrid Azure AD joined users - user cannot change display language themselves
rootCause: For Hybrid user scenarios, the M365 display language is determined by the preferredLanguage attribute in the on-premises AD user object, which is not set by default and users cannot change it themselves
solution: Admin sets preferredLanguage attribute in the DC user object: 1) Open Users and Computers > View > Advanced Features. 2) Open user > Attribute Editor > uncheck Show only writable. 3) Set preferredLanguage (e.g., de, en-GB). 4) Force AD replication. 5) Run delta sync via Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta. 6) Verify in Entra ID user blade
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Client Applications/IWP/Windows 365 Web Client Display Language not set | windows365.microsoft.com

symptom: Thin client AVD connection fails with error: The app is trying to access a service 5a0aa725-4958-4b0c-80a9-34562e23f3b7 (Windows Virtual Desktop AME) that your organization lacks a service principal for
rootCause: Thin client AVD configuration is set to use Classic AVD (WVD) instead of ARM/V2 mode, and the tenant does not have a Classic AVD setup so the Windows Virtual Desktop AME service principal is missing
solution: Reconfigure the thin client AVD configuration to use ARM/V2 Workspace mode instead of Classic WVD. On Dell thin clients: change the AVD configuration setting from Classic to ARM/V2
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Client Applications/Thin Clients configuration

symptom: Windows App (Unified client) crashes or closes by itself with no errors - either immediately or after 3-4 minutes
rootCause: Critical URLs required by Windows App are blocked by proxy/VPN/firewall: go.microsoft.com and query.prod.cms.rt.microsoft.com
solution: 1) Collect Windows App logs from C:Users{username}AppDataLocalTempDiagOutputDirWindows365Logs. 2) Open Windows365-WebView-instance.log, scroll to bottom, find failing HttpRequests. 3) Match the thread number of the failure with the thread number of the URL request. 4) Look for retry count going up (0/5 → 1/5 → 2/5) as confirmation of network block. 5) Have customer unblock/bypass the failing URLs from proxies/VPNs/firewalls
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Client Applications/Windows App/Known Issues/Windows app closing | crashing with no errors | Windows OS Unified client | Thread number search

symptom: Windows 365 Business grace period (7 days) blocks provisioning of new Cloud PCs when existing ones remain in grace
rootCause: Extended 7-day grace period for W365 Business keeps old Cloud PC allocated, blocking new provisioning for the same user
solution: Use PowerShell script with Microsoft.Graph module (CloudPC.ReadWrite.All scope) to identify Cloud PCs in grace period and manually end grace period/deprovision via Graph API endpoint POST /cloudPCs/{id}/endGracePeriod. Alternatively check CPCD Action Diagnostic for VSB service plan with Draft->Success status sequence.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Grace Period/Grace Period for Windows 365 Business

symptom: Cloud PC enters grace period and gets reprovisioned after provisioning policy modification when using Group Based Licensing (GBL) resize with single group for both licensing and provisioning policy
rootCause: When GBL resize uses a single group for both licensing and provisioning policy assignment, the user is moved to a target group that belongs to a different provisioning policy. Since policy ID changes are not checked during Pending Resize, the CPC finishes resizing but remains mapped to the wrong policy. Any subsequent change to that second policy triggers policy re-evaluation → grace period → reprovisioning.
solution: Use distinct/separate groups for licensing and provisioning policy assignment during GBL resize. To diagnose: 1) Check CPCD CPC Availability for DeviceID to see Original vs Current Policy ID divergence. 2) Check CPCD Tenant Diagnostic provisioning policy tracking for group assignments. 3) Verify in ASC license changes (old SKU deleted, new SKU enabled) matching resize date. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/resize-cloud-pc#resize-a-single-cloud-pc-provisioned-with-a-group-based-license
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Grace Period/Grace Period troubleshooting scenarios/Grace Period followed by Reprovisioning - Provisioning Policy modification trigger

symptom: Cloud PC Move fails with error ResetComputerPassword_deploymentTimeout - move to another region fails during provisioning with DNS registration timeout (Event ID 8015)
rootCause: DNS configuration on the destination region Azure vNet was pointing to a decommissioned DNS server IP address, causing DNS registration and computer password reset to time out
solution: Remove the stale/decommissioned DNS server IP from the Azure vNet DNS configuration. After correcting DNS settings, retry the move operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Move a Cloud PC/Known Issues/Move fails with error: ResetComputerPassword_deploymentTimeout

symptom: Win32 and Store Winget apps show as Skipped in DPP (Device Preparation Policy) Report when Managed Installer policy is enabled on the tenant
rootCause: When Managed Installer is enabled, Intune delivers Win32/Winget apps after DPP phase completes, so they cannot be tracked in the DPP report
solution: Known behavior - apps will still get installed despite showing as Skipped in the report. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/autopilot/device-preparation/known-issues#win32-and-winget-applications-are-skipped-when-managed-installer-policy-is-enabled-for-the-tenant
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Autopilot DPP for Windows 365 Enterprise And Frontline Dedicated

symptom: Azure AD Device Object not removed (stale) when Cloud PC is deprovisioned or reprovisioned - Intune device object removed successfully but Entra ID device object remains
rootCause: The enterpriseregistration.windows.net endpoint is blocked by firewall or network policy, preventing the dsregcmd /leave command from completing the device unjoin from Entra ID (error 0x80072efd = WININET_E_CANNOT_CONNECT)
solution: Verify and unblock outbound TCP 443 traffic to enterpriseregistration.windows.net. Check Azure AD Audit Logs for Delete Device events. If no Delete Device event exists, check on-device Event Viewer logs (Application and Services Logs > Microsoft > Windows > User Device Registration - Admin) for Event ID 233/204/314 indicating blocked endpoint. Have customer unblock the IPs for enterpriseregistration.windows.net.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Azure AD Device Object no removed (stale) at Deprovisioning or Reprovisioning Error: none

symptom: Cloud PC provisioning fails with PowerShell Constrained Language Mode error during Hybrid Join scenario - Intune enrollment output shows 'Definition of new types is not supported in this language mode'
rootCause: WDAC policy, AppLocker, GPO, or Intune policy is enforcing PowerShell Constrained Language Mode on the Cloud PC, which prevents the provisioning scripts from executing properly
solution: 1) If GPO/Intune policy is applying Constrained Language Mode, disable it and test. 2) If WDAC policy does not have 'Option 11 Disabled:Script Enforcement' set, open a Collab with WDAC team to enable it. 3) Check for third-party tools (Cisco, Zscaler) that may also enforce language mode restrictions.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/CPC integrate with WDAC

symptom: Cloud PCs are provisioned twice - multiple computer objects appear in Active Directory; Cloud PC shows 'Provisioned' then immediately moves back to 'Provisioning' state
rootCause: User is added to multiple groups (e.g., provisioning policy group and local admin group) in quick succession. The system detects mid-provisioning changes and forces a reprovision to ensure all requirements are applied before first sign-in. This is by design.
solution: Add the user to the local admin group (or other supplementary groups) either 1-2 hours before or after adding the user to the provisioning policy group, to avoid triggering a mid-provisioning change detection.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Cloud PCs are provisioned twice

symptom: Cloud PC deprovisioning fails to unjoin device from AD with error AzureCompute_VMAgentStatusCommunicationError - device objects left enabled in AD and Entra ID after deprovisioning
rootCause: Proxy configuration (e.g., Zscaler, Forcepoint) on the Cloud PC is blocking outbound connections to Azure, preventing the VM agent from communicating status and executing the AD unjoin (Dep_0_ureg_aduj) operation
solution: Configure proxy bypass for Azure/Windows 365 endpoints. Implement recommended Zscaler policy settings per: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/windows-365/optimizing-rdp-connectivity-for-windows-365/m-p/3554327. Verify outbound connectivity to Azure storage endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Deprovisioning fails to unjoin device from AD. Error: AzureCompute_VMAgentStatusCommunicationError

rootCause: The WinRE (Windows Recovery Environment) partition is enabled on the Cloud PC and positioned to the right of the C: drive. Windows can only expand a volume using unallocated space to its right, but the WinRE partition blocks this expansion.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Local disk didnt increase after resizing the Cloud PC (due to the winre)
solution: Option 1 (Manual): Run DISKPART to select and delete the WinRE partition (DELETE PART OVERRIDE), then REAGENTC /DISABLE, then extend C: drive from Disk Management. Option 2 (Bulk via Intune): Deploy Proactive Remediation script (ExpandDisk_withWinRE_Removal) that detects and remediates affected devices. Always create a manual restore point before performing either action.
symptom: After resizing a Cloud PC, the increased storage is not reflected within the machine. The C: drive does not expand despite successful resize in Intune. Disk Management shows unallocated space but it cannot be used to extend C: drive.
Cloud PC provisioning fails with error CreateVM_managedServiceIdentityNotFound when customer is onboarding to CMK (Customer Managed Key) feature
rootCause: Azure Disk Service failed to bind Managed Service Identity (MSI) with Disk Encryption Set (DES) when creating DES resources. DES is created per tenant per region, so if DES creation fails, all CPCs in that region are affected.
solution: 1) Engage W365 ConfigurationNetworkStorage team (CMK owner) via ICM to remove incomplete DES records and resources. 2) Customer retries provisioning — new DES will be auto-generated. Note: CMK is in public preview.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Provisioning fails with error: CreateVM_managedServiceIdentityNotFound

symptom: Cloud PC computer name shown on MEM/Intune Portal changes after resize, restore, move, or apply SSO operations
rootCause: By design. If customer renames the Cloud PC inside the VM or in AAD, the CPC service is not notified. However, when resize/restore/move is triggered, the service detects the changed name and updates the DB record.
solution: This is by design behavior. The computer name in MEM Portal will be updated to match the actual VM name after any resize/restore/move/apply SSO action. Microsoft will not accept requests to revert the name in DB.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/The computer name shown on the MEM Portal changed after resize, restore, move, apply SSO

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning fails with error HybridAzureADJoin_hybridAADJ_ScpMisconfigured or 0x801c001d. AD Configuration Test shows FAIL [0x8007003a]. DRS Discovery and subsequent tests are SKIPPED.
rootCause: Service Connection Point (SCP) object is not configured or misconfigured in on-premises Active Directory. The SCP azureADName/azureADId may not match the tenant used for provisioning, or SCP is missing entirely when using Entra Connect method.
solution: 1) Verify SCP exists by running LDAP query on DC: New-Object System.DirectoryServices.DirectoryEntry with path LDAP://CN=62a0ff2e-97b9-4513-943f-0d221bd30080,CN=Device Registration Configuration,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=domain,DC=com. 2) Confirm azureADName and azureADId match the provisioning tenant. 3) If empty, configure SCP via Entra Connect or verify targeted deployment GPO. 4) Ensure OU is within Entra Connect sync scope. 5) Verify AD Sites and Services subnet-to-DC mapping.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Provisioning fails during Entra Hybrid join phase. SCP Misconfigured or Discovery failure

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC resize precheck fails with 'The requested feature is not supported' or 'Resize blocked'. F12 trace shows resize is blocked.
rootCause: Resize precheck function (ResizePrecheckService.cs → BlockResizeForGen1VM) detects the Cloud PC as Gen 1 or the Trusted Launch value is null/empty, causing the precheck to return 'Not Supported' even if the VM is actually Gen 2.
solution: 1) Use CPCD CPC Availability section to confirm VM generation. 2) If CPC shows Gen 2 but Trusted Launch value is null/empty, this is a backend data issue → open ICM. 3) Gen 1 CPCs cannot be resized — this is by design. 4) Use Kusto query with ResizePrecheckService/UpgradeOrchestrator ComponentName to identify root cause.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Resize/Resize Precheck Failing with feature not supported. Resize blocked. Resize cannot be done at this time

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC snapshot/restore point is marked as Unhealthy status after being captured (manual or automatic)
rootCause: At the time of snapshot capture, both the VMAgent (GuestAgent) was not ready AND the device health status was unavailable. The snapshot health is determined by two API checks: VMInstanceView (Azure VM graph API) for GuestAgent status and DeviceHealth (Cloud PC graph API) for device availability.
solution: The unhealthy status is suggestive only and does NOT block any actions (export, restore, or share). Verify GuestAgent status via CloudPCEvent where ComponentName=="DeviceHealthCheckService" looking for DisplayStatus=="Ready". Check device health via ComponentName=="CheckConnectivityService" looking for Status=="Available". If both were temporarily unavailable during snapshot, the restore point may still work despite the unhealthy label.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Restore/Unhealthy Restore Point (Snapshot)

symptom: After removing Citrix connector from Intune, existing Cloud PCs still show 'Citrix Licensed' or 'Citrix Installed' state for 7 days before agent is removed and state is cleared
rootCause: By design: 7-day grace period exists after Citrix connector removal before the Citrix agent is uninstalled from CPCs and the license/install state is cleared
solution: This is expected behavior - 7 day grace period. Check external partner connector state via Kusto: cluster('cpcdeedprptprodgbl.eastus2').database('Reporting').fn_GetPartnerOrganizationEntity with PartnerId='198d71c0-80bb-4843-8cc4-811377a49a92' (Citrix) and TenantId. State visible in All CloudPCs list per CPC. Note: Kusto has 1-day delay.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Partner Connectors/Citrix HDX Plus/Citrix License State - Citrix Connector and Kusto check

symptom: HP Anyware agent not enabled correctly on Cloud PC - HP Anyware client cannot connect after initial enablement
rootCause: HP Anyware agent installation or onboarding process failed during initial provisioning
solution: Remove HP license from Windows 365 node in HP admin console → wait 10 min for offboarding → re-add HP license → wait 20-30 min for onboarding → retry HP Anyware client. If still fails, contact MSFT support for HP agent installation failure diagnosis.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Partner Connectors/HP Anyware/General/General Troubleshooting Tools

symptom: VMware BLAST not enabled correctly on Cloud PC after onboarding
rootCause: BLAST onboarding process failed or did not complete during initial VMware agent installation
solution: Remove VMware license from W365 node in VMware Cloud admin console → wait 10 min → re-add VMware license → wait 20-30 min → retry BLAST connection. If still unsuccessful, contact MSFT support for BLAST installation failure diagnosis. For RDP troubleshooting, add user to Local Administrators to bypass BLAST RDP block.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Partner Connectors/VMWare Blast/General/General Troubleshooting Tools

symptom: Connection Quality (Performance) report in Intune missing RTT, available bandwidth, and remote sign-in time details for some connections
rootCause: By design - no RTT data is collected when the connection duration is less than 2 minutes
solution: If connection > 2 min and data still missing, verify required endpoints are allowed without SSL inspection: *.prod.warm.ingest.monitor.core.windows.net, gcs.prod.monitoring.core.windows.net, *.events.data.microsoft.com, *.blob.core.windows.net, *.table.core.windows.net, *.servicebus.windows.net. Test reachability with Invoke-WebRequest and curl from the CPC.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Reporting/Connection Quality (Performance) report missing details on entries. Missing RTT bandwidth

symptom: Performance (Preview) blade in Intune showing partial data or no data for Windows 365 Cloud PCs
rootCause: Required telemetry endpoints blocked or SSL inspected, Connected User Experience service not running, or devices not enrolled in Intune Endpoint Analytics
solution: 1. Allow endpoints without SSL inspection: *.prod.warm.ingest.monitor.core.windows.net:443, gcs.prod.monitoring.core.windows.net:443, *.events.data.microsoft.com:443. 2. Verify Connected User Experience service is running. 3. Check WVD Events in Application.evtx for missing reporting endpoints. 4. Enroll devices in Intune Endpoint Analytics and create Windows Health Monitoring policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Reporting/Performance Blade Information

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC reports returning no data in Intune portal - no device details visible
rootCause: Azure Connected Machine agent (Azure Arc) was installed on Cloud PCs, causing mismatch between AVD session host and W365 entity device information, preventing report data aggregation
solution: Uninstall the Azure Connected Machine agent from affected Cloud PCs and reboot. The Azure Arc agent is not supported on Cloud PCs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Reporting/The reports for Windows 365 Cloud PCs are returning no data in Intune

symptom: Semantic File Search not working on AI-enabled Cloud PC when nested virtualization (Hyper-V) is enabled
rootCause: Nested virtualization conflicts with Semantic File Search on Cloud PCs
solution: Disable Virtual Machine Platform, Windows Hypervisor Platform, and all Hyper-V features via Control Panel > Turn Windows features on or off, then restart Cloud PC
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Settings Framework/AI-enabled Cloud PCs/Troubleshooting

symptom: User Settings local admin policy not applied - user is not local admin on Cloud PC despite User Settings policy being configured in Intune
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/User Settings policy/User Settings Local Admin - Check last run result and retry last payload
solution: 1) Check C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension\1.10.15\RuntimeSettings for .settings file containing localadmin PS1 URL. 2) Check matching .status file in Status folder for execution result. 3) If .status shows error: fix underlying issue (unblock URL/resolve policy conflict), delete .status file (NOT .settings), open admin cmd, cd to extension folder, run resetState.cmd then enable.cmd. 4) If no .settings/.status files exist after hours: networking issue on customer side.
rootCause: CustomScriptExtension failed to download or execute the local admin PS1 script. Common causes: blocked URL (saprod.infra.windows365.microsoft.com), conflicting dual policies with opposite local admin settings, networking issues preventing .settings file delivery, or PowerShell execution restrictions

symptom: Black screen after Windows Login on Windows 365 Boot device - user logs in and sees black screen with only pointer active
rootCause: Boot policy misconfiguration: Boot-to-Cloud fails so user stays local, but Lightweight shell policy applies which prevents local shell from loading. Typically OS edition mismatch or outdated Windows App/HostApp versions.
solution: 1) Validate Intune policy assignments: Enable Windows 365 Boot Desktop = Enabled, Override Shell Program = Apply Lightweight shell. 2) Confirm OS edition: Windows 11 Pro/Enterprise. 3) Ensure app versions: Windows App >= 2.0.285, HostApp >= 1.2.4159. 4) If unresolved: Reimage device or reapply Boot guided scenario.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Boot/Known issues

symptom: Windows 365 Boot sign-in fails after 2-minute transition/interstitial screen timeout. User sees transition screen, W365 App launches but Cloud PC connection never completes before the screen times out.
rootCause: Cloud PCs in inactive state (unused for a while) require 2-3 minutes to start. The transition screen has a fixed 2-minute timeout which expires before the VM is ready, resulting in a 30-second error screen followed by return to sign-in.
solution: Known platform limitation — VMs put in inactive state for resource management need startup time exceeding the transition screen timeout. Windows 365 and AVD App teams are aware. Kusto: query WindowsCoreEvents with CorrelationId, look for CPCStateChanged showing oldState/newState stuck at Connecting.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Boot/Troubleshooting Guide/Case Studies

symptom: Windows 365 COGS Optimization device actions (hibernate/resume/stop/start) fail, Cloud PC becomes inaccessible after optimization operation
rootCause: Internal service errors during COGS optimization device actions (hibernate, resume, stop, start, resize). Not all Cloud PCs are hibernate-capable; stop-start used for zero-utilization devices.
solution: 1) Ask customer to Restart the machine. 2) If Restart fails, request lockbox and call OCE API to Deallocate/Stop then Start. 3) If device cannot start from hibernated state, raise IcM to CloudPC Service / Cloud PC Cost Optimization team. 4) To cancel scheduled COGS workflows or exclude tenants, raise IcM to same team via SaaF triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Dedicated/CPC Optimization

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs are unexpectedly logged off during active sessions. Event ID 40 (reason code 2) in TerminalServices-LocalSessionManager, RdpCoreCDV channel closed events, force logoff due to critical failure in session.
rootCause: Cylance PROTECT Script Control blocking PowerShell scripts used by Cloud PC provisioning (CustomScriptExtension). Cylance was the last application accessed prior to logoff event per procmon analysis.
solution: 1) Check event logs for Event ID 40 reason code 2 and RdpCoreCDV channel close events. 2) Use Kusto: RDPCoreTSEventLog filtered by ActivityId for logoff messages; RDOperation for session notification details. 3) Check CustomScriptHandler.log for CylancePROTECT Script Control errors. 4) Temporarily disable Cylance on Cloud PC to confirm. 5) Customer works with Cylance vendor to resolve. Applicable to any third-party security tool blocking scripts.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Dedicated/Frontline Cloud PCs are unexpectedly logged off

symptom: Frontline Cloud PC concurrency status report not visible or showing empty in Intune admin center
rootCause: Admin missing required permissions: SharedUseLicenseUsageReport and SharedUseServicePlans roles not assigned in Tenant administration > Roles.
solution: 1) Assign SharedUseLicenseUsageReport and SharedUseServicePlans permissions via Tenant administration > Roles > Create > Windows 365 role. 2) If still empty, collect browser DevTools network trace of getSharedUseLicenseUsageReport request and check for errors. 3) Verify report page shows Current connections, Graph view, and Table data.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Dedicated/Frontline CloudPC Concurrency Status Report

symptom: Autopilot Device Preparation Policy (DPP) fails or is not available for Frontline Shared (FLS) Cloud PCs using Windows 10 images
rootCause: Device Preparation for Frontline Shared only works with Windows 11 images. Windows 10 images are not supported for this feature.
solution: Use a Windows 11 image in the provisioning policy when using Autopilot Device Preparation with Frontline Shared Cloud PCs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Shared/Autopilot DPP for Frontline Shared Cloud PCs (FLS)

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Shared provisioning policy with more than 10,000 users in assigned group - users beyond 10,000 cannot access or connect to Cloud PCs
rootCause: Known platform limitation: maximum 10,000 users per assignment group for Frontline Shared
solution: Split users into multiple groups (each <= 10,000) and create separate provisioning policies for each group
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Shared/Known Issues

symptom: Cloud Apps stuck in Preparing status after Cloud PCs successfully provisioned and DPP completed
rootCause: Policy assignment issue preventing Cloud Apps discovery from completing
solution: Delete and recreate the policy assignment
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Shared/Cloud Apps Support for Intune Autopilot DPP-FLS/Troubleshooting

symptom: Reprovision button in Windows 365 Frontline Shared provisioning policy does not work
rootCause: Known product limitation - Reprovision button requires the policy to be edited first
solution: Edit the provisioning policy first, then click the Reprovision button
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Frontline/Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared

symptom: Cannot purchase or activate W365 FedRAMP licenses after removing GCC licenses; Cloud PCs deleted during transition
rootCause: Mutual exclusivity of GCC and FedRAMP offerings at tenant level; Commerce system requires 72+ hours for GCC license deprovisioning
solution: Wait 72+ hours after GCC license deletion. Verify GCC licenses fully removed. Purchase and activate FedRAMP licenses. Use Kusto queries to validate offboard/onboard status.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Government/Downgrade from GCC to FedRAMP

symptom: GCCH government customers using web clients (especially Safari) cannot connect to their Cloud PC session when resources are deployed to microsoft.us environment
rootCause: microsoft.us cookies are treated as third-party cookies in web browsers. Safari blocks 3P cookies by default, and other browsers/group policies may also block them.
solution: Work with administrator to unblock 3rd party cookies for microsoft.us domain at browser level or Group Policy level. Non-web clients are not affected by this issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Government/Known Issues/GCCH government customers whose resources are deployed to microsoft.us environment, who utilize web clients or the  Safari browser may not get their client session

symptom: Windows 365 Government Cloud PCs cannot be configured on Microsoft Dev Box
rootCause: Dev Box is an unsupported environment for Windows 365 Government offerings
solution: Dev Box is not supported for Government Cloud PCs. This is documented at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/introduction-windows-365-government#features-not-yet-supported-windows-365-government
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Government/Support Boundaries

symptom: W365 Enterprise for GCC customers see Autopatch/Additional Services UX in provisioning policy but get error stating Autopatch can't be configured until a Global Admin has set it up
rootCause: Autopatch/MMD only supports commercial licenses and tenants; not applicable for W365 Enterprise for Government customers
solution: Select 'None' for Additional Services option to continue creating the provisioning policy. This UX will eventually be hidden for GCC tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Government/Windows 365 Enterprise for GCC Customers

symptom: Logs too large for ICM attachment limit when working with Government Cloud PCs; PG cannot access DFM case
rootCause: ICM has attachment size limits; PG does not have DFM access
solution: Add PG/Dev directly to DTM workspace and share DTM URL in ICM. See guides/drafts/ado-wiki-w365-gov-dtm-log-sharing.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Government/Windows 365 Government Guidelines and Troubleshooting/GCC GCCH ICM/How to Share logs in DTM workspace with Dev_PG
Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Entra/Conditional Access MFA device registration failure
solution: Create a Conditional Access policy in Entra admin center targeting Register or join devices user action with Require authentication strength: Multifactor authentication grant control. Apply to All users (exclude break-glass accounts). This ensures MFA claims are present during W365 Link device registration.
rootCause: Conditional Access policies enforcing MFA are applied to Cloud PC resources but a matching CA policy for the user action Register or join devices is missing, so the authentication token lacks required MFA claims after initial sign-in
symptom: Windows 365 Link device shows error An authentication issue occurred where an interactive window could not be shown during device registration when Conditional Access MFA policies are configured for Cloud PC resources but not for device registration/join action

sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Entra/Kerberos Server Object Requirement for Hybrid SSO when using Link
solution: 1) Enable SSO in the provisioning policy for the Cloud PCs. 2) For Hybrid Entra Joined CPCs: configure a Kerberos Server Object on each domain and forest per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/configure-single-sign-on. 3) For Entra Joined CPCs: KSO is only required if SSO to on-prem AD resources is needed; without it users get credential prompts for AD resources but CPC access still works.
rootCause: Windows 365 Link requires Entra ID SSO to be enabled on the Cloud PC provisioning policy. For Hybrid Entra Joined CPCs, a Kerberos Server Object must also be configured on each domain/forest containing Entra users. Without KSO, SSO authentication to the CPC (which is an AD resource) fails.
symptom: Windows 365 Link device shows error Your Cloud PC does not support Entra ID single sign-on when user attempts to connect to Cloud PC

Windows 365 Link device shows 'An authentication issue occurred where an interactive window could not be shown' during sign-in when Conditional Access MFA is configured for cloud resources but not for device registration
rootCause: Conditional Access policy enforcing MFA on cloud resources (Windows 365, Graph) is not matched by a corresponding policy on the user action to register or join devices. The sign-in token lacks the required MFA claims after initial authentication.
solution: Create a Conditional Access policy enforcing MFA on the user action 'Register or join devices': Entra admin center → Protection → Conditional Access → New policy → Target resources → User actions → Register or join devices → Grant → Require authentication strength → Multifactor authentication. Ensure this policy targets All users with appropriate exclusions.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Entra/Conditional Access MFA device registration failure

symptom: Windows 365 Link device experiences multiple reboots after installing 4B update
rootCause: Security baseline provisioning package included in 4B applies new policies at first user sign-in, requiring up to two automatic restarts after ~10 minutes of user inactivity to make the security configuration effective.
solution: This is expected behavior by design. The device will restart at most twice during idle periods (~10 minutes of no keyboard/mouse interaction) after the 4B update. No action needed - the restarts will stop after the security baseline is fully applied.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/NXT OS/Multiple reboots after installing 4B in Windows 365 Link

symptom: Windows 365 Link device stuck in recovery mode after Intune reset - WinRE boot loop
rootCause: Starting NXT OS preview build 26100.2161 (24-10D), Intune reset drops the device from the system with no way to re-enroll outside of OOBE. When device tries to boot into WinRE for wipe/reset, it enters recovery mode loop.
solution: Issue resolved in NXT OS build 26100.3194 (25-2B) for GA customers. For preview devices that cannot take the update, device replacement is required.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/NXT OS/WinRE Reset Issue

symptom: Windows 365 Link device enrollment fails with 'The Mobile Device Management (MDM) server doesn't support this platform or version, consider upgrading your device'
rootCause: Device platform restriction policy blocks personally owned devices, which prevents Windows 365 Link (classified as personal) from completing Intune enrollment.
solution: Create a higher-priority Windows enrollment restriction policy: Intune admin center → Devices → Enrollment → Windows → Device platform restriction → Create restriction → Allow MDM and personally owned devices → Assign to All users with Include filter for Windows 365 Link. Ensure the new policy is above the block policy in priority order.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Intune/Device Enrollment Restriction Failure

symptom: Windows 365 Link fails to connect to Cloud PC with error: Failed to open a Microsoft Entra ID credential prompt. Occurs on first SSO-enabled connection or every 30 days/after reprovisioning.
rootCause: Windows 365 Link cannot display the Entra ID SSO consent prompt that is required on first connection, every 30 days, or after reprovisioning. The device has no mechanism to show the interactive consent dialog.
solution: Suppress SSO consent prompt by: 1) Create dynamic device group for all Cloud PCs (deviceModel starts with Cloud PC), 2) Enable Entra authentication for RDP on SSO service principals (set isRemoteDesktopProtocolEnabled=true for AppIds a4a365df-50f1-4397-bc59-1a1564b8bb9c and 270efc09-cd0d-444b-a71f-39af4910ec45), 3) Add Cloud PC group to targetDeviceGroup on both service principals.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Entra/SSO Consent Prompt Failure

symptom: Windows 365 Link device displays time zone in PST by default; incorrect time zone shown on device
rootCause: NXT OS determines time zone from BIOS time and defaults to PST due to privacy restrictions; location-based time zone detection requires explicit privacy policy
solution: Create Intune Settings Catalog policy: Privacy → 'Let Apps Access Location' = 'Force Allow', assign to Windows 365 Link devices via device filter. Time zone will remain PST until device receives the policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Intune/Enable Link to Detect Time Zone Based on Location

symptom: Windows 365 Link device reboots unexpectedly during workday after OS update
rootCause: Intune update ring deadline expired (+ grace period ended), or Auto reboot before deadline policy allowed restart outside active hours
solution: Review and adjust Intune Update Ring: set appropriate deadline days, grace period, and active hours matching business workday. Collect Intune Diagnostic Logs to verify effective Update CSP values.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/NXT OS/Update Behavior

symptom: Windows 365 Link device displays time zone as PST by default out of the box regardless of actual location
rootCause: NXT devices default to PST time zone due to privacy restrictions - the OS determines time zone based on BIOS time which defaults to PST
solution: Create Intune configuration policy: Devices > Configuration > New Policy > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog. Search for Privacy, set Let Apps Access Location to Force Allow. Assign to Windows 365 Link devices using a device filter. Device will update timezone after receiving the policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/Intune/Enable Link to Detect Time Zone Based on Location

symptom: Windows 365 Link display settings (duplicate/extend/rearrange/resolution/scale/orientation) not available in Settings > Display
rootCause: Cloud PC OS not updated to required build version (26100.4333 for 24H2 or 22621.5469 for 23H2 or later) which includes the new blended display settings feature
solution: Update NXT Cloud PC to build 26100.4333 (24H2) or 22621.5469 (23H2) or later. If auto-update is enabled, settings appear automatically within a month. If on correct build but settings still missing, verify SystemSettingsProxy DLL exists at C:\Program Files\Microsoft RDInfra\SystemSettingsProxy. If DLL is missing, raise ICM to SaaF.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Components/New Display Feature

symptom: After installing WindowsCPC security update KB5066835 (October 14, 2025), USB devices (keyboards, mice) do not function in Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) on Windows 365 Link devices, preventing navigation of recovery options. USB devices continue working normally in the regular OS.
rootCause: The October 2025 security update (KB5066835) introduced a regression that breaks USB device driver loading in the WinRE environment on Windows 365 Link devices.
solution: 1) Use Intune remote wipe to reset the device instead of WinRE recovery. 2) If the device is stuck in WinRE, follow the escalation process to PCA Commercial team for hardware replacement. 3) Permanent fix delivered in the November 11, 2025 KB update from the NXT OS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/KNOWN ISSUES/WinRE Reset

symptom: After installing Windows 365 Link CPC security update (Oct 14, 2025 KB5066835), USB devices (keyboard and mouse) do not function in Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE), preventing navigation of recovery options
rootCause: NXT OS security update KB5066835 (released Oct 14, 2025) broke USB device support in WinRE environment while devices continue to work normally in the Windows operating system
solution: Use Intune remote wipe instead of local WinRE recovery. If device is already stuck in WinRE, escalate for replacement device via PCA Commercial queue. Fix delivered in November 11, 2025 KB update from NXT OS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/KNOWN ISSUES/WinRE Reset

symptom: Windows 365 Link shows error Something went wrong - An authentication issue occurred where an interactive window could not be shown after sign-in
rootCause: Authentication flow fails to display interactive window on Windows 365 Link device
solution: Follow documented troubleshooting at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-365/connection-error-interactive-window-not-shown
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/Error: Something went wrong

symptom: Windows 365 Link keyboard language reverts back to English after device goes to sleep
rootCause: Known bug in Windows 365 Link firmware prior to 4B update causing keyboard language reset on wake
solution: Workaround: Go to Quick Settings > About this device > Check for updates. Install and restart to apply 4B update which contains the fix
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/Keyboard language

symptom: Windows 365 Link OS update fails with error 0x80073712 and CbsPackageServicingFailure2 in Application Event Log
rootCause: Wrong Language Pack loaded on Windows 365 Link device causes update package staging failure
solution: Perform a remote wipe of the Windows 365 Link device via Microsoft Admin Center using Intune Wipe method. Important: Save BitLocker recovery key before reset
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/OS Update Failures

symptom: Windows 365 Link preview device fails to reset/wipe and becomes unusable (versions older than 2B 26100.3194)
rootCause: Known bug in Windows Recovery on preview image versions prior to 2B 26100.3194
solution: Do NOT reset/wipe devices on versions older than 2B 26100.3194. Update to 2B or later first. If already bricked, return device for replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/Preview Devices

symptom: Windows 365 Link preview device not automatically checking for updates overnight
rootCause: Known bug in preview image prior to 2B 26100.3194 that was not waking the device to check for updates
solution: Configure Intune Settings Catalog Policy with aggressive update check settings: Configure Deadline For Feature Updates=0, Configure Deadline Grace Period=1, Configure Deadline Grace Period For Feature Updates=1, Quality Update Deadline Period=0. Use Intune Filter for OS SKU WCPC. Fixed in 2B 26100.3194
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/Preview Devices

symptom: Windows 365 Link shows Securing your remote connection for ~2 minutes then returns to sign-in screen when Legal Notice/Login Message is configured on Cloud PC
rootCause: Known bug in preview image prior to 1B 26100.2894 preventing Legal Notice display on Windows 365 Link connection
solution: Remove Legal Notice/Login Message from Cloud PC as workaround. Fixed in 1B 26100.2894 or later
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/Preview Devices

symptom: Windows 365 Link shows Your clock is behind error, Advanced button does not work, and Update date and time does nothing
rootCause: Device screen timeout causes clock sync issue; manual time update not supported on Windows 365 Link
solution: Configure Intune Settings Catalog policy: Search for video and display settings, set Turn off the display (plugged in) value in seconds. Assign to Windows 365 Link devices using device filter
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Link/Troubleshooting Guide/Known Issues/Your clock is behind

symptom: Stale Cloud PC remains in Windows 365 Switch Task view after user loses access to it
rootCause: Previously added Cloud PC is no longer accessible but entry persists in Task view
solution: 1. Uninstall Windows App. 2. Reinstall Windows App. 3. Select 'Add to Task view' button on the Cloud PC you want to add.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Switch/Known issues

symptom: Bluetooth and hardware settings cannot be managed from Cloud PC Settings app or quick settings in Windows 365 Switch
rootCause: Cloud PCs lack hardware components like Bluetooth adapters, so these settings are not accessible from within the Cloud PC session
solution: Users must switch back to their physical device and change Bluetooth/hardware settings in the local Settings app.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Switch/Known issues

symptom: Reconnect button in disconnect message dialog does not work properly or results in unusable Cloud PC session in Windows 365 Switch
rootCause: Using the Reconnect option from disconnect dialog may fail to re-establish a working session
solution: Let the disconnect complete fully, then launch a new connection using Task view instead of using the Reconnect button.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Switch/Known issues

symptom: Cloud PC quietly disconnects while user is focused on local PC in Windows 365 Switch, showing black screen with message box
rootCause: Cloud PCs connected via Task view are designed to quietly disconnect to avoid disrupting the local PC session
solution: When the re-connectable error message is displayed, reconnect through the message box. This is expected behavior by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Switch/Known issues

symptom: After providing sign-in credentials when selecting Cloud PC from Task view, user is not returned to Cloud PC connection in some Windows builds
rootCause: In some builds of Windows, the sign-in prompt flow does not automatically navigate back to the Cloud PC connection
solution: Select the Task view button for the Cloud PC again. The connection continues in the background and user should be connected within a few minutes.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Switch/Known issues

symptom: Local PC missing from Cloud PC Task view bar in Windows 365 Switch
rootCause: Azure Virtual Desktop (HostApp) may be outdated, causing the local PC button to not appear in Cloud PC's Task view
solution: Uninstall and reinstall the Azure Virtual Desktop (HostApp) app from the Microsoft Store (ms-windows-store://pdp/?productid=9NRNM1N926MN).
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Windows 365 Switch/Known issues

symptom: Windows 7 WVD host pool deployment succeeds but VMs do not appear in host pool; RDP into VM fails with LogonUI.exe System Error: api-ms-win-core-winrt-error-l1-1-0.dll is missing from your computer
rootCause: Windows 7 ships with RDP 7.1 which is incompatible with WVD. Deployment without upgrading to RDP 8 and .NET Framework 4.7.2 causes VM registration failure.
solution: Before adding Windows 7 VMs to WVD host pool: 1) Install RDP 8 update (KB2592687), 2) Ensure .NET Framework >= 4.7.2, 3) Enable RDP 8.0 policy (Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Remote Session Environment > Remote Desktop Protocol 8.0), 4) Reboot VM, 5) Reinstall WVD Agent and Agent Manager with proper Registration Key. Note: Windows 7 is out of support.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Api-ms-win-core-winrt-error-l1-1-0.dll is missing from your computer

rootCause: Domain trust between the session host VM and the domain controller is lost, causing the I_NetLogonControl2 health check to fail
solution: Use TestDCName.exe diagnostic tool on the affected VM to simulate the API call and verify domain trust status; if trust is broken, rejoin the VM to the domain or repair the secure channel
symptom: AVD session host VM reports Unavailable status due to domain trust health check failure; I_NetLogonControl2 API call fails indicating secure channel is broken, preventing user authentication
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Domain Trust

rootCause: Bug in FSLogix versions prior to v2210 where RW differencing disk expansion logic did not handle SizeInMBs increase correctly
solution: Update FSLogix to version 2210 or later which includes the fix for proper RW differencing disk expansion
symptom: FSLogix RW differencing disks do not properly expand when SizeInMBs registry setting is increased from a smaller value
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_FSLogix v2210 Public Preview

solution: Update FSLogix to version 2210 or later which fixes profile lock retry count and intervals when Cloud Cache has the lock
symptom: FSLogix profile lock retry count and intervals do not work correctly when Cloud Cache holds the lock on the profile container
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_FSLogix v2210 Public Preview

rootCause: Bug in FSLogix versions prior to v2210 where impersonation for OneDrive permission setting failed when data was stored outside the profile path
solution: Update FSLogix to version 2210 or later which correctly impersonates OneDrive for setting permissions regardless of data location
symptom: FSLogix does not correctly set OneDrive permissions when OneDrive data is stored outside the user profile directory
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_FSLogix v2210 Public Preview

rootCause: When user disconnects during sign-out, the compaction process continues on the disconnected session; if user reconnects before compaction completes, the VHD(x) is still in use and FSLogix falls back to a temporary profile under default settings
solution: This is expected behavior; educate users to wait for sign-out to complete. For Cloud Cache configurations, be aware compaction causes longer sign-out as content must be downloaded from provider, compacted locally, then re-uploaded
symptom: User disconnects during FSLogix sign-out phase while disk compaction is running, then reconnects and gets a temporary profile instead of their persistent profile

symptom: MSIX App Attach: after user logs off a Full Desktop session while also having a Remote App session, attempting to relaunch the Remote App causes File Explorer to open instead of the expected application
rootCause: Logging off the Full Desktop session triggers a deregistration of MSIX packages, which breaks the Remote App session registration for the same user
solution: Log off all user sessions from the AVD portal after logging off the Full Desktop, then relaunch the Remote App
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Known Issues

symptom: MSIX App Attach: newly added MSIX application fails to launch (File Explorer opens instead) if user tries to open the app within ~30 minutes of adding it without rebooting the session host
rootCause: MSIX App Attach staging for packages only occurs during boot or approximately every 30 minutes during health checks, making it not fully dynamic
solution: Reboot the session host machine before trying to launch a newly added MSIX application, or wait up to 30 minutes for the next health check cycle
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Known Issues

symptom: x64 MSIX App Attach packages expanded to CIM image fail to open from start menu with error INVALID_PARAMETER 0xc000000d; x86 versions and VHD(x) images are not affected
rootCause: CIMFs driver bug affecting x64 MSIX packages when using CIM image format for App Attach
solution: Deploy MSIX packages as VHD(x) image instead of CIM format. Issue was fixed in Windows updates circa August 2022
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Known Issues

rootCause: UI publishing flow bug where all entry points in a multi-entry-point MSIX package incorrectly reference the last executable file defined in the AppxManifest, despite showing correct names and icons
solution: Issue was fixed in AVD service update (circa June 2021). Upgrade to latest AVD agent version

rootCause: Bug in CIMFs driver causing crash during MSIX CIM image mounting
solution: Issue was fixed by CIMfs team and backported. Update session host OS to latest patches (circa 2021)

rootCause: Registration race condition when multiple MSIX packages across different application groups are staged simultaneously during user logon
solution: Issue was fixed in AVD service update (completed 2021). Ensure session hosts are running latest AVD agent version

rootCause: Azure Stack HCI deployment service bug causing conflict errors when adding new session hosts
solution: PG deployed a hotfix to mitigate the issue. If still occurring, check for latest Azure Stack HCI updates. Related ICM: 510399439

symptom: SSO fails when subscribing from Remote Desktop macOS client - after entering UPN, user is redirected to another authentication box stating their tenant is not present in the system. Windows client SSO works fine.
rootCause: OneAuth library issue affecting macOS Remote Desktop client SSO authentication flow

symptom: Cannot create new Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) tenant after receiving UEX Azure subscription - tenant creation is blocked due to trial subscription restrictions
rootCause: Microsoft restricted Entra ID tenant creation to only paid subscriptions. If tenant was not created before receiving the UEX subscription, creation is blocked afterwards.

See: General Availability - Restrict Microsoft Entra ID Tenant Creation To Only Paid Subscription

rootCause: Modifying redirections mid-session is unsupported by design. Redirection state is determined at connection time based on Conditional Access authentication context claims
