symptom: AVD Insights workbook (new, not Insights Legacy) not selecting the expected Data Collection Rule (DCR) – wrong DCR selected, incorrect data displayed
rootCause: The default Insights workbook selects DCR based on naming convention 'microsoft-avdi-*'; a DCR that does not follow this naming convention will not be selected. The workbook looks for DCRs starting with 'microsoft-avdi-' prefix.
solution: Ensure the desired DCR name follows the 'microsoft-avdi-*' naming convention. The configuration workbook creates DCR as 'microsoft-avdi-<location>' by default. Rename or recreate the DCR with a name starting with 'microsoft-avdi-'.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Insights/Insights Workbook not selecting expected DCR

symptom: AVD Insights workbook not selecting the expected Log Analytics workspace – showing data from wrong workspace
rootCause: Default Insights workbook selects LA workspace based on alphanumerical sort of Diagnostic Settings; the first Diagnostic Setting that starts with numbers or letters AND has a LA workspace as destination wins. Diagnostic Settings starting with special characters (e.g., '_') are deprioritized. Priority order: allowed symbols → numbers → letters.
solution: Rename/recreate the desired Diagnostic Setting to appear first in alphanumerical sort (must start with numbers or letters). Note: Diagnostic Setting names cannot be edited – must delete and recreate with the correct name.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Insights/Insights Workbook not selecting expected Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AVD connection refused with error: 'Connection was refused because you tried to access a private endpoint resource without being connected to the private endpoint. Please retry after connecting to the private endpoint.'
rootCause: The host pool network access policy is set to private endpoint only (public access disabled, PrivateLinkID=2), but the client is attempting to connect via a public endpoint rather than through the configured private endpoint.
solution: 1) In ASC go to Host Pool > Connection tab, find the connection. 2) Copy activity ID, go to Tracing tab, paste activity ID, Tracing Type: Operation, Run. 3) Add Filter: Props contains PrivateLinkID — value 2=Disabled means public access is blocked. 4) Change Tracing Type to Server to see 'connection failed with access denied because network access from public endpoint is not allowed'. 5) Either have customer connect via the configured private endpoint, or adjust the host pool network access policy to allow public access.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/PL/Connection Failure

symptom: Workspace feed/subscription fails with error: 'The resource in <workspace name> can't be accessed from this network'. Users unable to subscribe or enumerate AVD resources via workspace feed.
rootCause: The workspace network access policy is configured to disallow connections from public endpoints (private link only). The client is connecting via a public endpoint, which is denied by the policy.
solution: 1) In ASC go to Workspace > Workspaces Feed tab and find the failed feed operation. 2) Copy activity ID, go to Tracing tab, paste activity ID, Tracing Type: Service, Run. 3) Add Filters: Category contains WebFeedLogic AND Msg contains Access. 4) Confirm feed request failed with 'access denied because network access from public endpoint is not allowed'. 5) Check workspace network access settings — either configure the client to connect via private endpoint, or update the workspace to allow public endpoint access.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/PL/Feed Failures

symptom: Customer cannot configure or use AVD Private Link; Private Link option not available, feature not visible in portal, or Private Link not functioning for workspace/host pool even after configuration attempts.
rootCause: Customer's Azure subscription has not been enrolled in the AVD Private Link Public Preview. Without enrollment, the Private Link feature is not available to the subscription.
solution: 1) In ASC select customer subscription > Azure Activity Logs. 2) Set appropriate time range, Filter: ResourceId contains AllowPrivateEndpoints. 3) If results found in logs, customer IS enrolled. If no results, customer is NOT enrolled. 4) To enroll: guide customer to Portal > Preview Features (https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Resources/PreviewFeaturesBlade) > search 'Private' > select 'Azure Virtual Desktop Private Link Public Preview' checkbox > click Register (bottom-right). 5) Enrollment may take time to propagate before Private Link features become available.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/PL/TSG/Enrolled in Preview

symptom: User session UPNs not displayed in AVD portal user sessions list; UPN column is empty or missing for active sessions despite users being connected.
rootCause: User subscribes to the AVD workspace with one set of credentials (e.g. User1@domain.com) but connects to a resource using a different set of credentials (e.g. direct1@domain.com or account from a different domain). This 'switched user credentials scenario' causes the orchestration layer to lose the UPN mapping.
solution: 1) Get ActivityID for an affected connected user session. 2) Run Kusto query on RDInfraTrace (all regions union) with ActivityId filter and Msg contains 'switched user credentials scenario' to confirm. 3) On session host, run 'quser <sessionID>' to get username. 4) Run 'Get-WmiObject win32_useraccount | Where-Object{$_.Name -like "<Username>"} | Select name,sid' to get SID. 5) Run '([ADSI]"LDAP://<SID=<User SID>>").UserPrincipalName' to get the actual UPN. 6) Compare with orchestration trace using connection ActivityID (filter on OrchestrationService and RDmiAuthorizationService categories) to confirm credential mismatch. 7) Inform customer to use consistent credentials for workspace subscription and resource connection.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Portal Not Showing UPNs

symptom: RDP Shortpath (UDP transport) fails to establish for client connections; AVD falls back to TCP transport only. Client side UDP is not being initiated.
rootCause: Client machine GPO policy is blocking UDP traffic, preventing RDP Shortpath from sending/receiving UDP ICE candidates. Confirmed when no UdpICEReceivedCandidates events exist in RDPCoreTSEventLog for the ActivityID.
solution: Run Kusto query: cluster('rdsprod.eastus2.kusto.windows.net').database('WVD').RDPCoreTSEventLog | union cluster('rdsprodus.eastus2.kusto.windows.net')... | where ActivityId == '<id>' | where TaskName == 'UdpICEReceivedCandidates' | project TIMESTAMP, Level, HostInstance, ProviderName, TaskName, EventMessage, Message. If no events are returned, UDP is disabled on client via GPO. Investigate client GPO settings and modify to allow UDP traffic to AVD STUN server IP ranges (13.107.17.41, 13.107.64.0/18, 20.202.0.0/16, 52.112.0.0/14, 52.120.0.0/14).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/RDP Shortpath Public/Kusto

symptom: RDP Shortpath (UDP transport) fails to establish from session host VM side; UDP ICE negotiation not initialized on the VM even when client UDP may be functioning.
rootCause: Session host VM GPO policy is blocking UDP traffic, preventing RDP Shortpath from initializing on the VM. Confirmed when no UdpICEInitialized events exist in RDPCoreTSEventLog for the ActivityID.
solution: Run Kusto query: cluster('rdsprod.eastus2.kusto.windows.net').database('WVD').RDPCoreTSEventLog | union cluster('rdsprodus.eastus2.kusto.windows.net')... | where ActivityId == '<id>' | where TaskName == 'UdpICEInitialized' | project TIMESTAMP, Level, HostInstance, ProviderName, TaskName, EventMessage, Message. If no events are returned, UDP is disabled on the VM via GPO. Investigate session host GPO settings and modify to allow UDP traffic for RDP Shortpath.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/RDP Shortpath Public/Kusto

symptom: Session Host Update (SHU) fails with error ImageHyperVGenerationDoesNotMatchExistingVms_1024: 'Image requires a HyperV generation V2 incompatible with the HyperV generation V1 associated with existing VMs in the Host Pool.' Customer attempts to update session hosts using a Gen2 gallery image but the host pool was originally deployed with Gen1 VMs.
rootCause: HyperV generation mismatch between the selected update image (Gen2/V2) and the existing session host VMs (Gen1/V1). AVD Session Host Update cannot change the VM generation in-place.
solution: Select an image that matches the HyperV generation of the existing VMs in the host pool. If existing VMs are Gen1, use a Gen1 image for the update. To migrate from Gen1 to Gen2, the host pool must be recreated with new Gen2 VMs rather than using in-place session host update.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/Failed - GEN1 to GEN2

symptom: Session Host Update fails with DeniedAccessToCustomerResource / AuthorizationFailed: 'The client does not have virtualMachines/write or disks/write permission.' Update reaches UpdateFailed state with faultCode DeniedAccessToCustomerResource.
rootCause: The AVD service principal used by Session Host Update is missing required RBAC permissions. Specifically, 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/write' and/or 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/write' were removed from the custom role assigned to the AVD SHU service principal (appid: 9cdead84-a844-4324-93f2-b2e6bb768d07).
solution: Restore the required RBAC permissions on the custom role: ensure 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/write' and 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/write' are included. Verify the AVD SHU service principal (oid: fccec22d-e6c5-4844-8ef1-1838c8b7cb8b) has the correct role assignment on the resource group containing the host pool.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/Failed - RBAC Perms

symptom: Session Host Update fails with UnableToAccessKeyvaultSecret_1024: 'Unable to access Keyvault Secret for secret VM-Local-Administrator-Password. Keyvault request failed with Status 403 Forbidden, ForbiddenByPolicy - does not have secrets get permission on key vault.'
rootCause: The AVD SHU service principal lacks the 'Get' permission on Key Vault secrets. Only 'List' was enabled under Secret Management Operations; 'Get' was unchecked. The service principal (appid: 9cdead84-a844-4324-93f2-b2e6bb768d07) cannot retrieve the VM local admin password secret required for the update.
solution: In the Key Vault access policy or RBAC assignment for the AVD SHU service principal, enable both 'Get' AND 'List' under Secret Management Operations. 'Get' is required to read the VM-Local-Administrator-Password secret during Session Host Update.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/KV Failure

symptom: Session Host Update fails with MigrationMayExceedCapacityLimit_1024: 'Resources created during the update process may cause subscription limits to be exceeded for Total Regional vCPUs or StandardBSFamily vCPUs.' Update is blocked before new VMs are deployed.
rootCause: The subscription's vCPU quota (Total Regional or SKU family) is insufficient to accommodate the temporary additional VMs created during Session Host Update. SHU creates new VMs before deleting old ones, requiring headroom above current usage.
solution: Increase the vCPU quota for the affected subscription and region via Azure portal (Subscriptions > Usage + quotas > Request increase), or delete unused compute resources to free capacity. Ensure the quota limit exceeds projected usage by at least the number of VMs being updated simultaneously (maxVMsUnavailableDuringUpdate).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/KV Failure

symptom: During Session Host Update, the portal displays 'update is pausing' status for 40+ minutes with no apparent progress. Customer believes the operation is stuck or hung.
rootCause: SHU waits for active user sessions to drain before fully transitioning to paused state. The 'update is pausing' status persists while session hosts are in drain mode, which can take extended time depending on session activity and drain timeout settings.
solution: This is expected behavior. The update will eventually transition to fully paused once all active sessions have disconnected or drained. Advise customer to wait for the operation to complete. If stuck for several hours with no session activity, retry or cancel and re-initiate the update.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/Pause-Resume

rootCause: W365 app has its own custom connection monitor UI messages separate from standard AVD. AVD sends connection status to CPC, which W365 app then takes and displays using its own UI layer
solution: CPC engineer should create an incident with CPC PG to investigate. If CPC determines it is not a CPC issue, transfer the incident to AVD PG

symptom: W365 Cloud PC session connects to wrong feed URL (e.g., subscribes to PROD feed instead of GOV feed)
rootCause: CPC app incorrectly constructs the .avd file URI, causing msrdcw.exe to subscribe to the wrong feed URL. AVD host app uses .avd file launch mechanism (similar to URI launcher) and depends on CPC correctly constructing the URI
solution: Create an incident with CPC PG to investigate the URI construction issue. If CPC confirms it is not their issue, transfer to AVD PG

symptom: Customer requests ability to selectively enable or disable RAW printing for specific individual printer devices only
rootCause: Feature limitation: SmartRawPrinters RDP property does not support targeting individual printer instances; it is either global (all printers) or filtered by printer driver name prefix
solution: Inform customer that RAW printing is a global toggle only. Options: (1) SmartRawPrinters:s:* to enable for all printers, or (2) SmartRawPrinters:s:<DriverPrefix1>;<DriverPrefix2> to filter by driver name prefix. Cannot enable/disable for specific individual devices.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/RAW Printing Support/FAQ

symptom: RAW printing not working or experiencing issues with RAW printing redirection in AVD or W365 remote session
solution: No additional troubleshooting specific to RAW printing is needed. Follow the same TSG steps as: (1) Printer Redirection — see 'Printer Redirection on AVD from a Print Server' wiki page; (2) General Device Redirection — see 'Device Redirection Overview' wiki page
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/RAW Printing Support/Troubleshooting

symptom: Relative Mouse feature not functioning in AVD session - mouse does not behave as relative mouse
rootCause: RDP property `AllowRelativeMouseMode:i:1` not added to Host Pool
solution: In ASC, go to Host Pool > RDP Properties > confirm/add `AllowRelativeMouseMode:i:1`

symptom: Need to verify if Relative Mouse is being triggered successfully in an AVD session
solution: Use ASC Tracing tab with connection Activity ID (filter Task Name = RelativeMouse), or Kusto query on RDClientTrace `| where TaskName == 'RelativeMouse'` across all regional clusters. Alternatively run RelativeMouseSupportTool.exe on AVD VM (Ctrl+O to toggle); if Relative Mouse Movement field shows N/A, it is not enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Relative Mouse/Troubleshooting

symptom: TWAIN scanner not working or not detected in AVD/W365 remote session
rootCause: WIA (Windows Image Acquisition) driver selected instead of TWAIN driver, or scanner issues on local client endpoint
solution: 1. First validate scanner works E2E on local client using free TWAIN software (e.g. NAPS2). 2. In scanner application on session host, ensure TWAIN driver is selected (not WIA). 3. Confirm TWAIN redirection enabled on session host: `reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" /v fTWAINRedirectionEnabled /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f`. 4. Ensure Windows App >=2.0.415.0 or MSRDC >=1.2.6220 on client.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/TWAIN/Troubleshooting

symptom: Multiple TWAIN scanners attached to client endpoint; wrong or no scanner selected as default in AVD session
rootCause: No default TWAIN scanner configured on client device
solution: Set default TWAIN scanner via client-side registry: `reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\Client\TWAINSelectDeviceByName" /v 8000 /t REG_SZ /d "<scanner device name>" /f`
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/TWAIN/Troubleshooting

symptom: Session hosts intermittently lose access to FSLogix profiles on Azure Files share
rootCause: MCAPS policies periodically enforce disabling Public network access on storage account, breaking session host connectivity to FSLogix profile share
solution: Re-adjust Public network access on storage account: set to 'Enable from selected virtual networks and IP addresses' with session host vnet subnets included. Monitor for MCAPS policy enforcement that may reset this setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/[AVD Lab] 11 - [Optional] Set up FSLogix Profile for your users/[AVD Lab] 11.1 - [FSLogix] Set up a Storage Account

rootCause: Enrollment can take up to 1 hour after user clicks Enroll before status changes to Enrolled; no notification is sent when enrollment completes

symptom: Customer enrolled in AVD per-user access pricing but sees no charges on their Azure subscription
rootCause: (1) Connections before April 1, 2022: expected — promotional period with $0 charges. (2) Connections after April 1, 2022: subscription may not be fully enrolled, or the AVD host pool object is in a different subscription than the enrolled one
solution: (1) Confirm connections occurred after April 1, 2022. (2) Verify subscription enrollment status is 'Registered' via Get-AzProviderFeature. (3) Confirm the AVD host pool resource is inside the enrolled subscription, not a different subscription
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/PCY or Region Specific Process Information Content/STA-EEE Corner/Centennial/RemoteApp Streaming/Technical Solution

symptom: Need to verify whether an Azure subscription is enrolled in AVD per-user access pricing
solution: Run PowerShell: (1) Connect-AzAccount (2) Get-AzProviderFeature -FeatureName 'PerUserAccessPricing' -ProviderNamespace 'Microsoft.DesktopVirtualization'. Check RegistrationState: NotRegistered/Unregistered=not enrolled, Registering=in progress (~1hr), Registered=enrolled, Unregistering=unenrolling
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/PCY or Region Specific Process Information Content/STA-EEE Corner/Centennial/RemoteApp Streaming/Technical Solution

symptom: VMs fail to start or stop during autoscale operations
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: incorrect scaling plan configuration, missing RBAC permissions on subscription, service-side issues, or VMs excluded via maintenance tagging
solution: 1) Verify scaling plan configuration is correct; 2) Confirm RBAC permissions for autoscale service on subscription; 3) Check for maintenance tags on VMs excluding them from scaling; 4) Review RDOperation Kusto logs for RDScaling failures

symptom: One session host always remains running even when scaling plan is configured to shut down all VMs during off-peak/ramp-down hours
rootCause: By design: if an active, disconnected, or pending session exists on a VM, Autoscaling will always consolidate to the fewest hosts possible but will never reach zero while a session is present
solution: This is expected behavior documented in the public FAQ (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/autoscale-faq). Educate customer that zero session hosts requires all sessions to be terminated first
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/PCY or Region Specific Process Information Content/STA-EEE Corner/Centennial/Autoscale/Technical Solution

symptom: Applications using AVD ARM API versions older than 2021-02-01-preview fail to retrieve RegistrationToken from GetHostPool API after August 2024 breaking change
rootCause: Security bug fix: GetHostPool API no longer returns RegistrationToken for old API versions (< 2021-02-01-preview). A new POST method RetrieveRegistrationToken was introduced in newer APIs.
solution: Update to API version 2023-09-05 or newer. Use the new RetrieveRegistrationToken POST method. For ARM/Bicep templates: use GET then PUT/PATCH (Create/Update) which returns RegistrationToken as write operations. See https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-virtual-desktop/update-to-microsoft-desktop-virtualization-api-v-2023-09-05-by/m-p/4180665

symptom: AVD/RDP connection fails during TLS handshake - network trace shows CLIENT HELLO but no SERVER HELLO response, indicating cipher suite mismatch between RDP client and server
rootCause: Cipher suite mismatch due to SSL Cipher Suite GPO or manual registry changes restricting available ciphers on client or server side. Registry key: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography\Configuration\SSL\00010002
solution: 1) Check gpresult on both client and server for SSL Cipher Suite GPO. 2) If GPO found, disable it and reboot to test. 3) Check Schannel registry keys for manually disabled ciphers: HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Ciphers and CipherSuites. 4) Update GPO so both sides can agree on cipher suites.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Certificates and Schannel/Check if there is a cipher mismatch (network trace analysis example)

symptom: AVD session connection shows Internal Error when TLS 1.1 and TLS 1.2 are disabled on the session host or client, leaving only TLS 1.0 available
rootCause: TLS protocols disabled via registry at HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols, typically by GPO or script. When TLS 1.2 is disabled, connections fail if the other side does not support TLS 1.0.
solution: Re-enable TLS 1.2 by modifying Schannel registry: set Enabled=1 and DisabledByDefault=0 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Client (and Server). See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/security/tls/tls-registry-settings
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Certificates and Schannel/Confirm if a specific TLS version is being disabled on the server

symptom: AVD session host deployment fails with dscextension error during DSC (Desired State Configuration) extension execution - typically occurs after VM is domain-joined when installing AVD agents and registering to host pool
rootCause: Configuration issues preventing proper communication between VM and WVD service during DSC phase. Common causes include network connectivity to required endpoints, WinRM configuration, proxy settings, or NSG rules blocking traffic.
solution: 1) Use ASC Resource Explorer -> Microsoft.Compute -> VM -> Extensions -> Microsoft.PowerShell.DSC to see failure reason. 2) Collect logs: C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Powershell.DSC\<version>\Status, C:\Windows\Temp\ScriptLog.log, DSC/PowerShell/WinRM event logs. 3) Verify connectivity to required WVD endpoints (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/safe-url-list). 4) Check for proxy: netsh winhttp show proxy. 5) Review NSG inbound/outbound rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Deployment Failures/Deployment fails with "dscextension" error

symptom: AVD deployment DSC extension fails because WinRM service cannot start or connect to remote endpoints - GPO applies WinRM configuration with empty IPv4Filter/IPv6Filter values
rootCause: Group Policy Object (GPO) configures WinRM service with undefined IPv4Filter and IPv6Filter, causing the service to listen on no IP addresses. WVD deployment scripts fail when trying to run Windows Remote Management tasks.
solution: 1) RDP to affected VM and run RSOP.msc or gpresult /h to check WinRM GPO. 2) Remove the problematic GPO or exclude AVD hosts from it (deploy to a separate OU). 3) Or modify GPO to properly configure WinRM IPv4/IPv6 filters. 4) Use MSRD-Collect diagnostic script which checks WinRM configuration including WinRMRemoteWMIUsers__ group, IP filters, firewall rules for ports 5985/5986.

symptom: AVD/RDS session host experiences server hang, slow performance, slow logons, black screen on login, or Start menu issues. Very large number of firewall rules accumulated under HKLM\SYSTEM\...\FirewallPolicy\RestrictedServices\AppIso\FirewallRules and IfIso\FirewallRules.

solution: Phase 1: Enable GPO "Delete cached copies of roaming profiles" to prevent future accumulation. Phase 2: Download FWCLEAN tools from https://aka.ms/fwclean and run on affected servers to clean up leftover firewall rules. Consider scheduling periodic cleanup.

symptom: Server or remote session hang, slow system/application performance, slow logons, black screen when logging in, Start menu issues on Windows Server 2016/2019 RDS/AVD servers or Windows 10/11 multi-session VMs. Large number of registry keys accumulated under FirewallPolicy RestrictedServices AppIso FirewallRules and FirewallPolicy RestrictedInterfaces IfIso FirewallRules.
rootCause: Firewall rules accumulate in the AppIso and IfIso registry keys over time as different users log on. Rules are not purged when user profiles are deleted, causing registry bloat.
solution: Phase 1: Enable Group Policy 'Delete cached copies of roaming profiles' (Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > System > User Profiles) to prevent future accumulation. Phase 2: Download FWCLEAN.EXE + FWCLEAN.PS1 from https://aka.ms/fwclean, run on each affected server to clean up leftover rules. Consider scheduling periodic cleanup via scheduled task.

symptom: AVD session host is unavailable. Application event log shows Event ID 3277 from WVD-Agent with description INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or EXPIRED_MACHINE_TOKEN. Registry key HKLM SOFTWARE Microsoft RDInfraAgent IsRegistered has value 1 but should be 0.
rootCause: VM was left off/deallocated for more than 90 days. The AVD agent token certificate is valid for 90 days and VMs need to be online for at least 20 minutes every 90 days to refresh tokens.
solution: Generate a new registration key in Azure Portal (Host pool > Registration key > Generate new key), then re-register the session host: 1) Set IsRegistered to 0 in registry, 2) Uninstall AVD Agent, 3) Reinstall AVD Agent with new registration token. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-desktop/troubleshoot-agent#error-invalid_registration_token-or-expired_machine_token
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/EXPIRED_MACHINE_TOKEN

symptom: AVD session host shows as Unavailable. Application event log shows Event ID 3277 with INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or EXPIRED_MACHINE_TOKEN. Registry key IsRegistered=1 but should be 0.
rootCause: VM was left off/deallocated for more than 90 days. The agent registration token certificate is valid for 90 days; VMs need to be online for at least 20 minutes every 90 days to refresh tokens.
solution: 1) Generate a new registration key from Azure Portal (Host pool > Registration key > Generate new key). 2) On the session host, run PowerShell as admin: Set-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent -Name IsRegistered -Value 0 -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent -Name RegistrationToken -Value <new-token> -Force. 3) Restart-Service RDAgentBootLoader. 4) Verify IsRegistered=1 and RegistrationToken is blank. Prevention: Keep VMs online for 20 minutes every 90 days.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/EXPIRED_MACHINE_TOKEN

symptom: Frequent DomainTrustCheck health check failures for AVD session hosts. Restarting the VM temporarily resolves the issue. Only specific hosts in the host pool are affected.
rootCause: TCP port exhaustion caused by a process (e.g., OpenLens) occupying all ephemeral TCP ports without releasing them. This prevents the VM from communicating with the domain controller, causing domain trust checks to fail.
solution: 1) Check Application event logs for Tcpip warning 4231 (all TCP ports in use). 2) Check for Group Policy processing failures (event 1054) and Netlogon events 5719 (RPC server unavailable). 3) Open Task Manager Details tab, add Handles column, identify processes with thousands of handles. 4) Kill the culprit process to restore connectivity. 5) If Microsoft process - collab/transfer to relevant team; if third-party - customer contacts vendor.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/DomainTrustCheck/DomainTrustCheck keeps failing frequently

symptom: AVD session host DomainTrustCheck health check fails frequently. Restarting the VM temporarily resolves the issue but it recurs.
rootCause: TCP port exhaustion caused by a process (e.g., OpenLens) occupying all ephemeral TCP ports and not releasing them, preventing the machine from communicating with the domain controller.
solution: 1. Check Event Logs for Tcpip warning 4231 (ephemeral ports exhausted), Group Policy event 1054, and Netlogon event 5719. 2. Open Task Manager > Details > add Handles column to identify processes with abnormally high handle count. 3. Kill the culprit process to restore domain trust without rebooting. 4. If third-party software, contact the vendor for a fix/update. If Microsoft process, transfer to the relevant team.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/DomainTrustCheck/DomainTrustCheck keeps failing frequently

symptom: AVD rdp-sxs (WVD listener) not showing in qwinsta output on session host, indicating the AVD SxS Stack listener is not functioning.
rootCause: Required WVD components (Remote Desktop Agent Boot Loader, RD Services Infrastructure Agent, Geneva Agent, SxS Network Stack) are not installed or misconfigured. On Windows 10 2004+, the fReverseConnectMode registry value may be set to 0.
solution: 1. Run qwinsta (admin cmd) to check if rdp-sxs listener is present. 2. Verify in Programs and Features: Remote Desktop Agent Boot Loader, RD Services Infrastructure Agent, RD Services Infrastructure Geneva Agent are installed. 3. For Win10 pre-2004: also verify SxS Network Stack is installed. 4. For Win10 2004+: check registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\rdp-sxs - verify fReverseConnectMode=1 (if 0, change to 1 and reboot). 5. If components missing, reinstall WVD Agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/SxSStack/AVD Listener Check

symptom: AVD Agent fails to upgrade/install (e.g. version 1.0.2682.1500 or later). Application event log shows Method not found exception. Agent resets to old version after failed upgrade.
rootCause: .NET Framework version on the VM is too low (e.g. 4.7). The new agent requires .NET 4.7.2 or greater. Missing method exceptions are caused by incompatible .NET runtime.
solution: Upgrade .NET Framework to version 4.7.2 or greater on the session host VM. Download: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4054530
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/SxSStack/Agent Upgrade Failure Check

symptom: SxS Stack installation fails on AVD session host. VM status shows Unavailable or Upgrading. Manual or automatic stack installation fails with registry-related errors.
rootCause: DisableRegistryTools registry key is enabled (under HKU\.DEFAULT, HKU\S-1-5-18, or HKCU policies), preventing AVD components from editing the registry during SxS Stack installation.
solution: 1) Remove all DisableRegistryTools registry keys. 2) Uninstall the broken SxS stack from Apps & Features. 3) Check HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations and remove stuck rdp-sxs* keys. 4) Reboot VM. 5) Wait for AVD Agent to auto-reinstall SxS stack (1-2 minutes).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/SxSStack/SxS Stack Agent Stuck on Upgrading

symptom: SxSStackListenerCheck health check fails. Users get An error occurred while accessing this resource. Kusto shows ConnectionFailedReverseConnectStackIsNotListening. VM shows Unavailable.
rootCause: VM is running an unsupported OS SKU. AVD only supports client Enterprise SKUs and Server SKUs. Unsupported: Windows 10/11 Pro, 32-bit OS, N/KN/LTSC editions, Windows 7, Windows Server 2012 R2.
solution: Deploy a supported OS per AVD prerequisites: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/prerequisites. Verify OS via ASC Resource Explorer (Guest OS Name) or Kusto DiagActivity (AgentOSDescription).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/SxSStack/SxSStackListenerCheck

symptom: AVD host pool deployment fails with InvalidContentLink error: Unable to download deployment content from Storage Blob. Pasting the blob URL in browser returns 'storage blob does not exist'.
rootCause: ARM template artifacts are missing from the storage blob used by the AVD deployment pipeline.
solution: Create an ICM incident to the AVD PG team. PG will upload the missing ARM templates using the artifact deployment pipeline.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Deployment Failures/Error: Unable to download deployment content from Storage Blob

symptom: Session host health check failure: 'Unable to locate cert RDSAgentPROD.geneva.keyvault.RDSAGENT.WVD in store My from LocalMachine'. Event log source: Microsoft.RDInfra.Agent.Monitoring.GenevaCertificate, Log: RemoteDesktopServices.
rootCause: Geneva monitoring agent certificate is missing or invalid on the AVD session host.
solution: Follow the Geneva Monitoring Agent Check troubleshooting guide to remediate the certificate issue. Reference: https://eng.ms/docs/cloud-ai-platform/azure/aep-platform/sigma/sigma-remote-desktop-windows-virtual-desktop/internal-documentation/devops/documentation/sessionhost/healthchecks/monitoringagentcheck
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/Geneva Agent/Unable to locate cert RDSAgentPROD.geneva.keyvault.RDSAGENT.WVD in store My from LocalMachine

symptom: WVD Agent upgrade fails with 'Method not found' exception in Application event log. Agent reverts to old version after MSI shows successful installation. Affects upgrade to agent versions 1.0.2682.1500 / 1.0.2548.650X or later.
rootCause: .NET Framework version on the session host is below 4.7.2 (e.g., .NET 4.7), causing method incompatibility with newer AVD agent versions that require .NET 4.7.2+.
solution: Upgrade .NET Framework to version 4.7.2 or later: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4054530/microsoft-net-framework-4-7-2-offline-installer-for-windows
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Health Check Failures/SxSStack/Agent Upgrade Failure Check

symptom: AVD deployment fails with error InvalidTemplateDeployment / InvalidContentLink: Unable to download deployment content from Storage Blob. Pasting the blob URL in a browser returns 'storage blob does not exist'.
rootCause: ARM template artifacts are missing from the storage blob used by the AVD deployment pipeline.
solution: Create an ICM incident. PG will need to upload the missing ARM templates using the artifact deployment pipeline.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Deployment Failures/Error: Unable to download deployment content from Storage Blob

symptom: AVD session host fails to register with error INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN. Event ID 3277 in Application logs (Source: WVD-Agent). Registry key IsRegistered=0 at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent.
rootCause: The registration token used by the RD Agent has expired or is invalid.
solution: 1) Generate a new registration token from Azure Portal (Host Pool → Registration key → Generate new key). 2) On the VM, open registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent, set IsRegistered=0, paste the new token into RegistrationToken. 3) Restart boot loader: net stop RDAgentBootLoader & net start RDAgentBootLoader. 4) Verify IsRegistered changes and RegistrationToken is cleared.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN

symptom: AVD session host fails to register with error NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED. Event ID 3277 in Application logs (Source: WVD-Agent). Multiple VMs using the same session host ID.
rootCause: Custom image was created from a VM that was already registered in a host pool (agent installed and registered before sysprep). All VMs deployed from this image share the same session host ID, causing identity conflicts.
solution: 1) Remove the affected session host from the host pool (Azure Portal → Host Pool → Session Hosts → Remove). 2) Wait a few minutes for the session host to re-register with a new unique ID and show as Available. For prevention: follow AVD Custom Image Recommendations - uninstall agent before sysprep.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED

symptom: AVD agent process crashes on session host, Event ID 1000 in Application logs. Processes affected may include RdAgentBootLoader, RdpInit, RdpShell, Explorer, TermServ.
rootCause: Agent crash can be caused by underlying INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED issues (misleading crash), or a genuine agent bug requiring PG investigation.
solution: 1) Collect MSRD-Collect and check Event ID 1000s. 2) First verify the crash is not caused by INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED (these can generate misleading crash events). 3) Use Kusto query on RDPCoreTSEventLog where ProviderName=='LSM' and Message startswith 'szOutput="ERR::RCM process exit'. 4) If genuine crash, configure procdump to capture process dump on next crash, then create ICM for PG review.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Process Crashing

symptom: Users get repeated/duplicate sessions on the same AVD session host after disconnecting and reconnecting. Portal shows 2 sessions with the same SessionID but qwinsta shows different SessionIDs.
rootCause: Registry key fSingleSessionPerUser at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server is set to 0, allowing unlimited simultaneous remote connections per user on the same session host.
solution: Set registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\fSingleSessionPerUser to 1. Alternatively, enable GPO: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → Remote Desktop Services → Remote Desktop Session Host → Connections → 'Restrict Remote Desktop Services users to a single Remote Desktop Services session'.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Repeated sessions On Session Host VMs

symptom: AVD deployment fails with 'Unable to download deployment content from Storage Blob' and InvalidContentLink error when ARM template artifacts are missing from storage
rootCause: ARM templates artifacts missing from storage blob, causing deployment to fail when trying to download required content
solution: Create an ICM incident to PG team. PG will upload missing ARM templates using the artifact deployment pipeline
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Deployment Failures/Error: Unable to download deployment content from Storage Blob

symptom: AVD session host fails to register with INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN error. Event ID 3277 in Application logs (Source: WVD-Agent). Registry key IsRegistered=0 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent
rootCause: Registration token is invalid, expired, or corrupted. The RDAgent cannot authenticate with the AVD service (broker) using the current token
solution: 1) Generate new registration key: Azure Portal > Host Pool > Registration key > Generate new key. 2) On VM, navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent, set IsRegistered=0, paste new token into RegistrationToken. 3) Restart RDAgentBootLoader: 'net stop RDAgentBootLoader & net start RDAgentBootLoader'. 4) Verify IsRegistered becomes 0 and RegistrationToken is empty after successful registration. Use Kusto queries against RDOperation/RDInfraTrace tables with ResSignature/Msg has 'INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN' to correlate
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN

symptom: AVD session host registration fails with NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED error. Event ID 3277 in Application logs (Source: WVD-Agent). Multiple VMs sharing same session host ID
rootCause: Custom image was created from a VM that was already registered (agent installed and registered in host pool before running sysprep). Each RDAgent install generates a unique session host ID. If all VMs use the same ID from the golden image, the service cannot identify individual endpoints or route connections correctly
solution: Remove the session host from host pool: Azure Portal > Host Pool > Session Hosts > click 3 dots > Remove. Wait a few minutes, the session host should show available again. For prevention, follow AVD Custom Image Recommendations (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/set-up-golden-image#other-recommendations) - ensure agent is NOT registered before sysprep
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED

symptom: AVD agent process crashing with Event ID 1000 in Application logs. Processes affected: Winlogon, TermServ, RdAgentBootLoader, RdpInit, RdpShell, Explorer, Appx
rootCause: Agent crashes can be misleading - often caused by underlying INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED issues which generate Event ID 1000 crash events. If neither applies, the crash requires PG investigation via process dump
solution: 1) Collect MSRD-Collect and check Event ID 1000s. 2) First verify NOT hitting INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED (these generate misleading crash events). 3) Use Kusto: RDPCoreTSEventLog where ProviderName=='LSM' and Message startswith 'szOutput="ERR::RCM process exit' to find crash events. 4) If actual crash, configure procdump to capture process dump when agent crashes, then create ICM for PG review
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Process Crashing

symptom: AVD users get assigned to same session host but a new session is created on reconnect. Portal shows 2 sessions with same SessionID but qwinsta shows different SessionIDs for both connections
rootCause: Registry key fSingleSessionPerUser under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server has been changed to 0, allowing unlimited simultaneous remote connections and preventing session reconnection to existing sessions
solution: Set registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server [fSingleSessionPerUser] to 1. Alternatively, enable GPO: Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Connections > 'Restrict Remote Desktop Services users to a single Remote Desktop Services session'
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Host or AVD Agent/Repeated sessions On Session Host VMs

solution: See Microsoft documentation: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/troubleshoot-set-up-issues#error-deploymentfailed--invalidresourcereference (two scenarios documented)
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Workflows/Deployment Failures/Error: DeploymentFailed  InvalidResourceReference

solution: See Microsoft documentation: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/troubleshoot-set-up-issues#error-the-admin-username-specified-isnt-allowed

symptom: Windows 365 ANC check fails with error 'An account with the same name exists in Active Directory. Re-using the account was blocked by security policy' (error 0xaac) during canary VM domain join
rootCause: Domain join fails due to restrictive Remote SAM access policy configured on domain controllers. Default allows Everyone on DCs but customer restricted to Administrators only, blocking ANC service account validation
solution: Add permissions for the ANC service account in the Remote SAM access policy (Network access: Restrict clients allowed to make remote calls to SAM), or set the policy to default. Reference: KB5020276 - Netjoin domain join hardening changes
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Azure Network Connection/ANC failure: "An account with the same name exists in Active Directory"

symptom: Windows App client requires proxy support to be manually enabled via registry key (temporary optional requirement)
solution: Enable proxy support via registry key: Add DWORD 'EnableWindowsAppProxy' with Value 1 under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsApp\Flights or HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsApp\Flights
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Client Applications/Windows App/TEMPORARY Optional Requirement: Enable proxy support flighting

symptom: "No workspace is associated with this email address" error when subscribing with email in RDP client (MacOS). Feed discovery fails.
rootCause: DNS TXT record _msradc not configured for the user's email domain. The record is required for automatic workspace feed discovery via https://rdweb.wvd.microsoft.com/api/arm/feeddiscovery.
solution: In O365 Admin Center > Settings > Domains > DNS records, add TXT record: Name=_msradc, Value=https://rdweb.wvd.microsoft.com/api/arm/feeddiscovery, TTL=1/2 Hour. Wait 5-10 minutes then retry. If persists, check VPN/Firewall/content filter blocking the discovery URL. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-email-discovery
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Email Discovery: "No workspace is associated with this email address" when subscribing with an email address

symptom: Cannot connect to Cloud PCs using Citrix after reboot; machine shows unregistered, inbound traffic blocked by Windows Firewall. HAADJ environment. Issue is intermittent and sometimes resolves after another reboot.
rootCause: After reboot, network profile does not switch to Domain Authenticated and remains on Category: Public, Type: Unmanaged. Public firewall profile blocks inbound connections (Citrix, RDP agent registration, etc.). Visible in Security.evtx (Windows Filtering Platform blocks) and Microsoft-Windows-NetworkProfile-Operational.evtx.
solution: Check Microsoft-Windows-NetworkProfile-Operational.evtx to compare reboot timestamps. On failed boots, network profile stays Public instead of switching to Domain Authenticated. Investigate why domain authentication fails during boot (NLA service, DNS, DC reachability). Fix domain detection to ensure firewall profile switches to Domain after reboot.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Firewall profile: Cannot connect using Citrix | CPC inbound failing

symptom: Cannot connect to Cloud PC via RDP desktop client with error "Local Security Authority cannot be contacted". Web client works fine. System EVTX shows TLS encryption mismatch between client and session host.
rootCause: Customer modified TLS ciphers in registry, removing RSAE-PSS cipher suites. This causes a cipher mismatch between the client device and the Cloud PC session host during TLS negotiation.
solution: Add back the following TLS ciphers to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Cryptography\Configuration\Local\SSL\00010003 on BOTH client and CPC: RSAE-PSS/SHA256, RSAE-PSS/SHA384, RSAE-PSS/SHA512. The cipher settings must match on both sides.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/LSA Local Security Authority cannot be contacted: Cipher mismatch

symptom: CTRL+ALT+DEL and CTRL+ALT+END keyboard shortcuts trigger on Cloud PC instead of the remote server in a nested RDP session (MSTC from within Cloud PC to another server).
rootCause: By design, keyboard shortcuts like CTRL+ALT+DEL do not propagate through nested RDP sessions. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/termserv/terminal-services-shortcut-keys
solution: On the destination remote server, create a shortcut: C:\Windows\explorer.exe shell:::{2559a1f2-21d7-11d4-bdaf-00c04f60b9f0} and assign a custom shortcut key (e.g., CTRL+ALT+A). Also set the .RDP file keyboard setting to 'On the remote computer'. Shortcut must be created manually on each server (copy-paste does not carry the key combo).
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Nested RDP Keyboard Shortcuts: CTRL ALT DEL

symptom: Connection fails with error SavedCredentialsNotAllowed (error code 0x3): "The server denied the connection because the session host configuration does not allow using saved credentials". Fails when reconnecting to disconnected sessions.
rootCause: Registry key fPromptForPassword (HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services) is set to 1 on session host via GPO, requiring manual password entry. Users who checked Remember Me have saved credentials that are rejected.
solution: Workarounds: 1) Sign off disconnected users (issue only on reconnect). 2) Delete saved credentials from Windows Credential Manager on client (name: RDPClient:WkspId:<guid>; if not visible try: rundll32.exe keymgr.dll,KRShowKeyMgr). 3) On client device set HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\DisablePasswordSaving=1 to prevent saving. 4) On CPC set fPromptForPassword=0 (security implications). Known bug: https://microsoft.visualstudio.com/OS/_workitems/edit/42830991
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Saved Credentials: The server denied the connection because the session host configuration doesn't allow using saved credentials

symptom: RTT and bandwidth information missing from CloudPC Performance / Connection Quality blade. Geneva monitoring agent cannot reach monitoring endpoints due to SSL inspection.
rootCause: SSL inspection (by firewall/proxy) is intercepting traffic to Geneva monitoring endpoints, breaking the monitoring data pipeline.
solution: Run elevated PowerShell in Cloud PC: Invoke-WebRequest -Uri 'https://gcs.prod.monitoring.core.windows.net/healthcheck'. If it fails, disable SSL inspection for: *.eh.servicebus.windows.net, *.prod.warm.ingest.monitor.core.windows.net, gcs.prod.monitoring.core.windows.net.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Geneva Agent/Geneva Agent: Missing RTT and Bandwidth from Performance TAB

symptom: RTT and bandwidth information missing from CloudPC Performance / Connection Quality blade. Geneva Agent scheduled task is disabled or blocked by policy/third-party application.
rootCause: Geneva Agent scheduled task is not running - either disabled, stopped, or blocked by a policy or third-party application. The task runs under SYSTEM context.
solution: On affected CloudPC check: 1) Geneva Agent is installed. 2) Check EventViewer > RemoteDesktopServices for Geneva Agent errors. 3) Verify Geneva Agent Scheduled Task is running and not Disabled. Enable task History and reproduce to detect if task is being stopped or denied. Check for policies or 3rd party apps blocking SYSTEM-context task execution.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Geneva Agent/Geneva Agent: Missing RTT and Bandwidth from Performance TAB

symptom: ReverseConnectInvalidCertificate error when connecting to AVD session host. Proxy/tunnel IP appears in Kusto traces (X-ProxyUser-IP) that is different from client IP and VM IP, but basic diagnostics do not show proxy involvement.
rootCause: A third-party network appliance (e.g. Palo Alto firewall/proxy) intercepts the reverse connect traffic between the session host and AVD gateway, causing a certificate mismatch. The proxy IP is visible in RDInfraTrace (Msg contains 'proxy') but not in basic DiagActivity.
solution: Diagnostic steps: 1) Query DiagActivity for error details (SessionHostIP). 2) Query RDPCoreTSEventLog (TaskName='HttpConnect') to verify gateway resolution. 3) Query RDInfraTrace (Msg contains 'proxy') to find X-ProxyUser-IP. 4) Check if the proxy IP is a Microsoft IP via csstoolkit.azurewebsites.net/Home/MicrosoftIPs. 5) If non-MS IP found, try accessing https://<IP> to identify the appliance. Customer must fix or remove the proxy/tunnel from the AVD connection path.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Known Issues/AVD: ReverseConnectInvalidCertificate masked proxy - tunnel IP

symptom: Azure AD authentication window loop: blank AAD login window appears and disappears in endless loop, then cycles back to 'configuring remote connection'. Users unable to connect to AVD session host. Error in stack trace: LsaLogonUser failed (rdsaadauthserver.cpp). Entra SSO / RDSAADAUTH is in use.
rootCause: The krbtgt_AzureAD account (and linked AzureADKerberos account) was deleted from Active Directory. The Domain Controller cannot decrypt the user's Partial TGT (McTicket) because the issuing account no longer exists, causing 'Server Principal Unknown' error.
solution: Restore the krbtgt_AzureAD account and associated AzureADKerberos account from the AD recycle bin for the affected domain. Once restored, the Domain Controller can decrypt the Partial TGT again and SSO logon resumes. Diagnostic: Check RDClientTrace for AAD operations (token obtained but logon fails), then RDPCoreTSEventLog for stack errors (LsaLogonUser failed, cloudaphelper.cpp). Engage Identity PG for simultaneous client/server auth trace if needed.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Known Issues/AVD: The user failed to authenticate. Azure Auth window loop blank.  krbtgt_AzureAD account deleted

symptom: X224SecFilterFailedActivate error during AVD connection. Users cannot connect. ErrorInternal=true, ErrorSource=Stack. x224 negotiation fails. Stack trace shows ActivateInternal failure in rdpencsecfilter.cpp related to certificate error.
rootCause: The RDP listener certificate is missing - the SelfSignedCertStore registry key at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations is empty. Without a valid certificate, the security filter cannot activate and x224 negotiation fails.
solution: Add 'Remote Desktop' as the value for the SelfSignedCertStore registry key at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations, then restart TermService (termsvc). Verify with MSRD-Collect: check Certificates-RemoteDesktop.txt to confirm certificate is present. Diagnostic Kusto: query RDInfraTrace (Msg contains 'cert') for 'RetrieveSerializedCertificate failed'; query RDPCoreTSEventLog for X224SecFilterFailedActivate and SecurityFilter events.
sourceRef: /Features/Dependencies/AVD/Known Issues/AVD: X224SecFilterFailedActivate

symptom: Windows 365 provisioning, COGS resume, Flex Start, or ANC checks fail with errors: AllocationFailed, "Insufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this region", ErrorAzureAllocationFailure, or ErrorResourceUnavailable.
rootCause: Azure capacity outage in the region where Cloud PCs are located. Impacts: COGS (deallocated/hibernated CPC cannot resume), Flex Start (Frontline machines cannot start), ANC checks (canary VM creation fails), Provisioning (new machine creation fails).
solution: For CSS: Check internally for capacity issues in affected region, inform SaaF Team with Kusto findings. DO NOT assume capacity error without checking Kusto. DO NOT suggest resize/move to customers. DO NOT inform customers about capacity errors unless agreed with SaaF and Dev team in active ICM. For SaaF: When alert fires, follow incident trigger playbook for specific scenario.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Windows 365 Capacity related Errors: Allocationfailed Insufficient capacity ErrorAzureAllocationFailure

symptom: Customer requests Microsoft Support to access their Virtual Hard Disks (VHDs) for troubleshooting Windows 365 Cloud PC issues.
solution: Decline the request. Support Engineers do not have access to customer VHDs and access cannot be granted. ICMs requesting VHD access will also be declined. Refer Enterprise/Frontline customers to: 1) Share restore points to storage: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/share-restore-points-storage 2) Place Cloud PC under review: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/place-cloud-pc-under-review

symptom: Cloud PC resize fails with error CheckVmAgentStatus_customScriptExtensionHandlerError; CustomScriptExtension handler shows 'Not Ready' for old version while newer version fails to install
rootCause: Stale registry key and folder for previous version of Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension remain on the Cloud PC, preventing newer version from installing during resize
solution: Connect to impacted Cloud PC as admin: 1) reg delete "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\HandlerState\Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension" /f  2) rd /s /q c:\packages\plugins\Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension  3) Retry resize which triggers CSE reinstall from snapshot

rootCause: Network connectivity issue preventing Cloud PC from accessing Windows 365 provisioning blob storage; 403 Forbidden indicates proxy/firewall intercepting and breaking SAS token authentication
solution: Investigate and fix network path between Cloud PC and blob storage URL (saprod.infra.windows365.microsoft.com). Remove proxy/firewall rules that modify or block requests to this endpoint

solution: Add missing system environment variables to PATH: %SystemRoot%\system32, %SystemRoot%, %SystemRoot%\System32\Wbem, %SYSTEMROOT%\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\, %SYSTEMROOT%\System32\OpenSSH\. Then retry restore

solution: Upgrade to W365 version 1.0.147.0 or newer. With this version, when both W365 and AVD apps excluded from CA policy, the CA policy will work for W365 APP.

symptom: Cloud PC shows black or white screen when connecting. Restart/reprovision does not help. Event ID 402 Kernel-PnP with 0xc0000361 STATUS_ACCESS_DISABLED_BY_POLICY_DEFAULT.
rootCause: GPO or Intune CSP policy blocking driver installations prevents the Indirect Display Driver from functioning on the Cloud PC
solution: Check for policies blocking driver installations via GPO or Intune CSP (DeviceInstallation). Check setupapi.dev.log and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceInstall\Restrictions. Remove/exclude blocking policy from Cloud PC devices.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Connectivity Error & TSG Mapping/Black or White Screen when connecting

symptom: Cloud PC shows black screen. RCM process exit in Kusto. Appx/Readiness suspected.
rootCause: AppReadiness tasks timing out during logon causing desktop to fail to render
solution: Add registry keys: (1) HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\AppReadinessPreShellTimeoutMs=60000 (2) AppReadinessGlobalTimeoutMs=120000 (3) HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\fRunAppReadiness=0. Reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Connectivity Error & TSG Mapping/Black or White Screen when connecting

symptom: Black screen then disconnect. Error 0x10b or 0x3 (CreateInputDevicesError). Winlogon Event ID 4005. Connection failed cant create Input devices in Kusto.
rootCause: DeviceInstallation CSP policies (PreventInstallationOfMatchingDeviceIDs etc.) deployed via Intune prevent RDP input device creation (keyboard/mouse)
solution: Get MDM report and GPResults. Identify DeviceInstallation CSP policies on Cloud PCs. Exclude these policies from Cloud PC device groups. Once synced, users can connect normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Connectivity Error & TSG Mapping/Black screen and Winlogon terminated event (4005) upon logon to Cloud PC

symptom: Cloud PC inaccessible. CPCD: AVDStatus=Unavailable, FailType=RunScriptTimeout. Heartbeats: Unable to connect. WaAppAgent.log: not enough space on disk.
rootCause: Cloud PC disk completely full preventing VM agent from functioning
solution: Option 1: Restore Cloud PC to time when disk was not full, then clean up. Option 2: Resize to higher SKU with larger disk.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Connectivity Error & TSG Mapping/Cloud PC in inaccessible because the disk is out of space

symptom: Frontline Cloud PC browser connection fails: You are using an invalid link to connect to a Cloud PC. Connect from Windows 365 instead. Event logs show cannot reach WireServer.
rootCause: ZScaler or proxy not configured to bypass WVD/AVD traffic and WireServer IPs 169.254.169.254 and 168.63.129.16
solution: Configure proxy to bypass: (1) WVD gateway URLs (2) WireServer IPs: 169.254.169.254 and 168.63.129.16. See safe-url-list docs.

symptom: Multiple users report performance drops, hangs and disconnections on Cloud PCs. Proxy configured. Kusto shows same non-Microsoft SessionHostIPAddress for many users.
rootCause: Proxy ignoring W365/WVD URL exclusions, routing all AVD traffic through proxy in different region
solution: Add required AVD URLs to proxy whitelist. Primary: *.wvd.microsoft.com. Full list: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/required-fqdn-endpoint

symptom: User gets error 'The system administrator has restricted the types of logon (network or interactive) that you may use' when connecting to Cloud PC. CPCD shows LogonTypeNotGranted or NotAuthorizedForLogon errors.
rootCause: Custom image had default groups (Administrators, Users, Everyone) removed from the 'Allow log on locally' policy under User Rights Assignment.

symptom: Wide screen monitor showing erratic or low frame rate feedback while using Windows 365 with Windows App. Gaming overlays (e.g. Nvidia GeForce Experience) detect very low FPS.
rootCause: RDP protocol uses dynamic framerate capped at 30fps by default. Static screen content does not trigger redraws, causing overlays to report 0 FPS.
solution: Increase RDP frame rate cap to 60fps via registry change (see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/remote/frame-rate-limited-to-30-fps). For Nvidia GPU users, enable 'Image Scaling' in GeForce Experience to force per-frame post-processing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Wide Screen Monitor is giving Erratic Feedback while using Windows 365 with Windows App

symptom: Black screen on Cloud PC connection followed by disconnection. Error codes 0x10b (ConnectionFailedReverseUngracefulClose) and 0x3 (CreateInputDevicesError). Event ID 4005: 'The Windows logon process has unexpectedly terminated'. RDPCoreTSEventLog: 'AddExistingDevice MAX_DEVICE_ENTRIES hit'.
rootCause: DeviceInstallation CSP policies (PreventInstallationOfMatchingDeviceIDs, PreventInstallationOfDevicesNotDescribedByOtherPolicySettings, etc.) deployed via Intune block RDP input device creation, causing Winlogon crash.
solution: Collect MDM report and GPResults. Identify DeviceInstallation CSP policies being delivered to Cloud PCs. Exclude those policies from affected devices or create proper AllowInstallation rules. After policy sync, user should connect without error. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/policy-csp-deviceinstallation
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Connectivity/Windows Logon crash. Error 4005 when connecting

symptom: Cloud PC provisioning fails with 'Not Enough IP Addresses' error when customer uses their own VNET (Bring Your Own Network / BYON).
rootCause: The subnet serving the Azure Network Connection (ANC) has exhausted all available IP addresses.
solution: Customer must extend the address space for the subnet serving the ANC in their VNET to provide more IP addresses for Cloud PC provisioning.

symptom: Cloud PC provisioning fails with 'Not Enough IP Addresses' error when using Microsoft Hosted Network (MHN).
solution: Reach out to SaaF Engineer and raise ICM with complete details: Tenant ID, provisioning policy details, failure timestamp. SaaF transfers ICM to ConfigurationNetworkStorage Team for investigation.

symptom: Cloud PC stuck in endless reboot loop with 'DSC is restarting...' dialog. Event 1074 shows DSC-initiated restart (Reason Code 0x80040001). Get-DscConfigurationStatus shows reboot pending.
rootCause: InstallLanguage.ps1 DSC script caught in reboot loop caused by customer ASR (Attack Surface Reduction) policies blocking DISM execution.
solution: For new machines: switch ASR policies to Audit mode. For existing machines: login during ~5 min window between reboots and run: Remove-DSCConfiguration -Stage Pending,Current,Previous -Verbose. Bug: https://microsoft.visualstudio.com/OS/_workitems/edit/44514277
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/DSC/DSC Reboot Loop

symptom: VoIP call not being redirected through Multimedia Redirection (MMR) even though browser extension shows as loaded. Observed with Salesforce calling pages.
rootCause: The actual calling URL (e.g. my.connect.aws used by Salesforce) was not registered in the MMR custom domain registry key. The visible page URL differs from the underlying WebRTC calling domain.
solution: 1. Install latest MMR from https://aka.ms/avdmmr/msi. 2. Use MMR extension Advanced Settings > Start Collect logs to identify actual calling URL. 3. Add all required domains to registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\3rdparty\extensions\joeclbldhdmoijbaagobkhlpfjglcihd\policy. 4. Restart browser. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/multimedia-redirection-video-playback-calls
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/AVD/Redirection/Multimedia Redirection (MMR)/Call not being redirected through MMR even if the extension shows up as loaded

symptom: When accessing \\tsclient\Windows365 virtual drive\ in Cloud PC web client, network discovery error: Network discovery is turned off
rootCause: Network discovery is not enabled on the Cloud PC by default when using web client file transfer
solution: Click Turn on network discovery and file sharing in the yellow banner. Then access \\tsclient\Windows365 virtual drive\ to find Uploads and Downloads folders.
